style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* ----------------------------<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">-------<span> </span></span>*/ </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// an individual rule targets <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em>PartUsage</em> metaclasses and applies the specified style to them. Because of the global rule, only part usages displayed on a General View will be affected by the rule</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#2A16FF&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Creating custom rules</h3><h4>Creating custom rules via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create a custom rule via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, s</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">pecify the rule, d</span>eclare a part usage, specify a name for the new rule, subset the <em>rule </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet </em>element, and in its body, do <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">any of the following as needed:</span><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying conditions for a rule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the rule's condition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying styles for a rule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the rule's style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3462d38f-58d9-4205-935c-b42fd15cadd7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStylesPackage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Styles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">::**;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreStylesComponents</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Predicates</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColorDefinitions</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerMLStyles</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysMLStyles</span>::*;</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* -------<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">new rule <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">-------</span></span><span> </span></span></span>*/</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c">part</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c">partRule</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c"> :&gt; </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c">ru</ac:inline-comment-marker>le</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">       <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Specify the rule's condition</span><br />               <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Specify the rule's style</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* ----------------------------<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">-------</span></span><span> </span></span></span>*/</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating custom rules via the style sheet symbol compartment in a view</h4><p>To create a custom rule via the style sheet symbol compartment in a view</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="043997db-810b-4742-89cf-e0d23cb035ab"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="5b57bb53-1a64-4dcc-bd21-e8049d94ee35">Create the <em>rule</em> part usage element for the style sheet's part definition element via the <strong>Create Element </strong>button by following the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating elements via compartments"><ri:page ri:content-title="Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating elements via compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure.<br />A rule is created with the <em>ByElementCondition </em>condition and <em>ShapeStyle </em>style, which includes redefinitions for <em>fillColor </em>(with a randomly assigned color value)<em> </em>and <em>penColor </em>(with a comparatively darker color value) attributes. You can modify them as needed, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying conditions for a rule" /> and <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying styles for a rule" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create rule.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8cbf90ff-e87a-42e3-b4b0-de3192421c94"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">part1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::ByElementCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#81c784&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">penColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#3e6140&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating custom rules via the Specification panel</h4><p>To create a custom rule via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="cc9a42e4-dde5-43b5-ae82-ede9fd8eda1e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="185be77e-4987-4f6c-8d3b-48395534aea8">Create the <em>rule</em> part usage element for the style sheet's part definition element via the Specification panel by following the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating an element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating an element via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure.<br />A rule is created with the <em>ByElementCondition (Specific Elements) </em>condition and <em>ShapeStyle </em>style, which includes redefinitions for <em>fillColor </em>(with a randomly assigned color value)<em> </em>and <em>penColor </em>(with a comparatively darker color value) attributes. You can modify them as needed, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying conditions for a rule" /> and <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying styles for a rule" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating custom rules via the Specification panel.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d9f6b832-784b-429d-b202-f1dd0f9850ed"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">part1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::ByElementCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#81c784&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">penColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#3e6140&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Specifying conditions for a rule</h3><p>You can specify the condition for a rule via the Textual Editor or the Specification panel. The names of the conditions differ based on the chosen specification method. See the comparison table below.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Condition Element Name in Textual Editor</th><th>Condition Name in Specification Panel</th></tr><tr><td>ByElementCondition</td><td>Specific Elements</td></tr><tr><td>ByElementMetaclassCondition</td><td>Element Kind</td></tr><tr><td>ByMetadataAnnotationCondition</td><td>Metadata Annotation</td></tr><tr><td>ByDiagramTypeCondition</td><td>View Type</td></tr><tr><td>FreeFormCondition</td><td>Custom Expression</td></tr></tbody></table><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For more information, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet rule conditions table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Style sheet rule conditions table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></span></p><h4>Specifying conditions for a rule via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify a condition for a rule via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part element of the rule, declare a part usage and r</span>edefine the <em>condition </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet</em> element, then define it by the needed condition.</li><li>Within the body of the condition, declare a <em>ref item</em>, then r<span>edefine the needed item </span><span>and specify its values.</span><ol><li data-uuid="eac01675-9514-4d75-bf05-2cb28b9f2b89">For the <em>ByElementCondition</em>, in its body, declare a <em>ref item</em> and redefine <em>dependencyOwner</em>. In its body, declare a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[dependency]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the rule's condition to the target element.</li><li data-uuid="324224b0-6b91-4f31-8bff-0fc1177b7e1d">For the <em>FreeFormCondition</em>, declare a <em>calc </em>and redefine <em>test</em>, then specify the needed expression.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66f6e464-a7c3-442a-a23a-47fbe9442747"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The available ref items and their value types depend on the condition definition you are using. For a list of the condition definitions and their available ref items, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet rule conditions table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Style sheet rule conditions table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54ec3c1e-f836-4456-9c0a-556a564fff5c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>ByElementCondition</em> example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::ByElementCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByElementCondition</em>, targeting a specific element symbol</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">dependencyOwner</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">dependency</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">from</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> Model::CustomStyleSheet::customRule::condition </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> vehicle;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets the <em>vehicle</em> element</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>ByElementMetaclassCondition</em> example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByElementMetaclassCondition</em>, targeting symbols by element metaclass </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets elements with the <em>PartUsage</em> metaclasses</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>ByMetadataAnnotationCondition </em>example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">metadataApprovalRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span> {<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByMetadataAnnotationCondition</span> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByMetadataAnnotationCondition</em>, targeting symbols by element metadata</span></span><br />      <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">annotatingMetadataType</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Vehicle Metadata Definitions'</span>::Approval.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets elements with the applied <em>Approval</em> metadata</span></span></span><br />   }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>ByDiagramTypeCondition </em>example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">generalViewRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span> {<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByDiagramTypeCondition</span> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByDiagramTypeCondition</em>, targeting symbols by view definition</span></span><br /><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">      ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">diagramType</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets views defined by the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em>General View </em>view definition</span></span></span></span><br />   }<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>}</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>FreeFormCondition </em>example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">MyFreeformRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FreeFormCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>FreeFormCondition</em>, targeting symbols by the specified expression</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">test</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         '</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">VCCU View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">diagram</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets element symbols on the specific view '<em>VCCU View</em>'<br />         // OR<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">         element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == VCCU.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets the <em>VCCU</em> element symbol on all views (to which the style sheet is applied)</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      <br />      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying conditions for a rule via the Specification panel</h4><p>To specify a condition for a rule via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="00415ffd-14b2-4106-9237-d0dc14b8479a"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the style sheet's <em>rule</em> part usage element.</li><li data-uuid="7588cd53-1a65-491b-b39d-309f586f9e44">Specify the condition in the Specification panel's <strong>condition </strong>compartment:<ol><li data-uuid="fec36ec4-733e-46d3-9f1b-d2910bee539c">Specify the needed condition in the <strong>Match by</strong> field. Click the field to view the dropdown menu with available conditions, then click the needed condition to specify it for the rule.</li><li data-uuid="035ff853-26f3-4643-a1a1-028e43db250b">Specify the condition values in the value field. The value field name depends on the specified condition:<br /><table class="wrapped" style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Condition</th><th>Value Field Name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td>Specific Elements</td><td>Element</td><td><p>Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or a view to specify the value.</p><p>What happens in the Textual Editor: The feature value of the condition's item redefines <em>dependencyOwner</em>.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td>Element Kind</td><td>Metaclass</td><td><p>Drag and drop a metaclass (e.g. metaclass from KerML library or metadata definition from SysML library) from the Containment tree or a view to specify the value.</p><p>What happens in the Textual Editor: The feature value of the condition's item redefines <em>elementMetaclass.</em></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td>Metadata Annotation</td><td>Metadata Definition</td><td><p>Drag and drop a metadata definition from the Containment tree or a view to specify the value.</p><p>What happens in the Textual Editor: The feature value of the condition's item redefines <em>annotatingMetadataType.</em></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td>View Type</td><td>View Definition</td><td><p>Drag and drop a view definition from the Containment tree or a view to specify the value.</p><p>What happens in the Textual Editor: The feature value of the condition's item redefines <em>diagramType</em>.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td>Custom Expression</td><td>Expression</td><td>Specify the expression in the Textual Editor.</td></tr></tbody></table><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="condition specification.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><h3>Specifying styles for a rule</h3><p>There are two ways you can specify a style for a rule:</p><ul><li>Local specification allows you to specify the style of a specific rule only.<ul><li data-uuid="2000a681-ca91-4dbb-b733-bbdd3631ae5e">See the procedures: <ul><li data-uuid="d123e334-483d-43ab-8daf-0811e33fc505"><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule" />.</li><li data-uuid="ab79d6b7-599f-4c61-81bd-07e233188de3"><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule via the Specification panel" /></li></ul></li></ul></li><li>External specification allows you to create a custom style definition outside the rule itself, which can then be reused for style specification of different rules.<ul><li data-uuid="9e725475-3662-4b9f-af5f-e2244d79587b">See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions" />.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify style attributes locally for the style of a rule via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part element of the rule, after the condition specification, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">declare a view usage, define it by the needed style definition, and </span>redefine the <em>style </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet </em>element.</li><li><span>Within the body of the </span><em>view<span> </span></em><span>element, </span><span>d</span>eclare an attribute usage, redefine the needed attribute and <span>specify its value.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2c2a0ef-0685-478d-9e4a-5f70ded74929"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>The available attributes depend on the style definitions <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(primary or custom) </span>used for the view's definition:</span><ul><li>For a list of primary style definitions, their attributes, and value types, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></li><li>To create custom style definitions, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions" /> procedure.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To define by multiple style definitions, list them in a comma-separated list.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce17c2fa-38b1-4524-9b03-d6abee3ebde3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span> {<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's style is <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">defined by a primary style definition <em>StyleShape</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FF0F37&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// local modification of an available attribute's value</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule via the Specification panel</h4><p>To specify style attributes locally for the style of a rule via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="09bd8c38-a6a9-4c9a-a803-caa891c31d20"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the style sheet's <em>rule</em> part usage element.</li><li data-uuid="9336d564-3d9f-440b-89ab-a7f35a668339">In the Specification panel's <strong>style attributes </strong>compartment:<ol><li data-uuid="674fd42d-5cbe-4a81-b399-3eff66cd9792">Specify the style definition in the <strong>Type </strong>field:<ol><li data-uuid="39dc42a2-eaa6-456f-89d9-6218b06f4a87">Click the input field to view the dropdown menu with available style definitions. Click the needed one to specify it as the type.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="style definitions dropdown menu.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="aeb8027f-4f3d-418b-b508-433512c37d5a">To remove the specified style definition, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete specified style definition button.png" /></ac:image> button next to the style definition.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete specified style definition.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="1f904e30-5774-4650-b4a2-22ce024dfc5e">To remove all specified style definitions, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="More button for style definition.png" /></ac:image>button in the <strong>Type</strong> input field, then click <strong>Clear All</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clear all types.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="ec5940b9-512e-4227-bff1-1400ac1ce77a">Specify the style attributes:<ol><li data-uuid="d5dd1484-6ff5-4e5d-8654-232f0d450575">Modify the owned attributes in the <strong>Owned</strong> section:<ol><li data-uuid="1af83061-774b-4faa-95cc-93c9bc9d9461">See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying element values via the compartment in the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying element values via the compartment in the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /></ac:link> page.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="4f148693-656f-46db-b93f-d537bd4fa63b">Redefine inherited attributes in the <strong>Inherited</strong> section and then modify them as needed:<ol><li data-uuid="a09c2387-525e-4821-902d-5b2ab0b6ae3d">See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining inherited features via the compartment in the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefining inherited features via the compartment in the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /></ac:link> page.</li><li data-uuid="5973747b-3205-44bc-bacd-96132a2cbf10">Then see the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying element values via the compartment in the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying element values via the compartment in the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /></ac:link> page.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds</h4><p>To hide style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8cf9a6dd-9439-4468-a94d-d3a142be4436"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part element of the rule, after the condition specification, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">declare a view usage, define it by the needed style definition, and </span>redefine the <em>style </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet </em>element.</li><li data-uuid="8d40055e-33d0-470c-abcf-c70e4f0ea1d4"><span>Within the body of the </span><em>view<span> </span></em><span>element, </span><span>d</span>eclare an attribute usage and apply the <em>InvisibleInDialog </em>metadata to it, then redefine the style attribute you want to hide from the Symbol Style dialog for the element kind specified in the rule's condition<span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="922934b4-4737-4b7b-a1b2-9b180f221f01"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>The available attributes depend on the style definitions <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(primary or custom) </span>used for the view's definition. </span>For a list of primary style definitions and their attributes, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</li><li data-uuid="14d743eb-5b86-41e9-86f0-1a15c1a8cbec">For more information on the Symbol Style dialog, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Modifying styles of individual symbols" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="4d20e455-c815-4318-87bc-6fe4f415e4f9"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18071fed-1f01-4d4f-98fd-7271fa24e1ba"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">rule</span> {</span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">     </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">#</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">InvisibleInDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">contentFillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the style attribute <em>Content Fill Color</em> is hidden from the Symbol Style dialog for part usages<br /></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating custom style definitions</h4><p>Unlike local attribute specification, creating custom style definitions allows you to reuse them for style specification of multiple rules. </p><p>You can create custom style definitions specifying the needed attributes and their values. <ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet composition"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Custom style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom style definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must subclassify <ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet composition"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Custom style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[primary style definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (such as <em>ShapeStyle, ShapeOnBorderStyle</em>, etc.), inheriting their attributes, which you can modify. However, you can only add attributes that are available to the primary style definition that the custom style definition subclassifies. <br />For an extensive list of primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></p><p>To create a custom style definition</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, d</span>eclare a view definition element, specify its name, and subclassify a primary style definition<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> from the </span><em>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">package. </span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="596add9c-8bf9-4461-ba72-634bce333c7d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a list of primary style definitions, their attributes, and value types, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the view definition element, d</span><span>eclare an attribute usage, r</span><span>edefine the needed attribute and specify the attribute's value.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41ce5236-0257-4e7a-a387-75d00f1383a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The available attributes and their value types depend on the primary style definition you are using. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For an extensive list of the primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Specify the custom style definition for the style of a rule.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8008acdb-37fd-4acc-aea6-817d5ce97e9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">custom rule</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span> : <u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span></u> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the style of the rule is updated to be defined by the new custom style definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">custom style definition subclassifies the primary style definition <em>ShapeStyle</em></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textColor</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#68FF70&quot;</span>;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating custom reusable style attribute values</h4><p style="text-align: left;">You can create custom style attributes whose values you can reuse by referencing them where needed. Additionally, you can create custom style classes grouping such attributes. </p><p>To create custom reusable style attribute values</p><hr /><ol><li><span>Declare an attribute, s</span>pecify its name, then define it by the needed value type, and s<span>pecify its value. </span></li><li>(Optional) To group attributes within a custom style class, declare a part usage, specify its name, then place the attributes within the part's body.</li><li>Reuse the newly created style attribute values for <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom style definition attributes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local attribute specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <span>Use <ac:link ac:anchor="Name resolution"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[feature chaining]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for attributes nested within <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom reusable style attribute values"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom style classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78d7dca6-fd32-48a1-8bdc-0a91345f6019"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part </span>partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the custom style is passed to the rule's style</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">penColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customColorPalette.customDarkGreen</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the custom color from a custom style class is reused in a custom style definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customColorPalette</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// custom style class with reusable style attributes for custom color values are specified</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Specifying the compartment style of a rule</h3><p>To specify the compartment style of a rule </p><hr /><ol><li><span>Within the body of the </span><em>view<span> </span></em><span>element, d<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">eclare a part usage, specify its name (optional), then d</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">efine it by the appropriate compartment style definition. </span><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb8c8880-e28e-45bb-9747-60e585fc5aca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To determine which primary compartment style definitions are available for each element symbol, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol compartment styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol compartment styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body, redefine the needed compartment style attribute followed by the appropriate attribute value. <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong> <br /></strong></span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="351a66c1-c268-4e8d-9290-29bd90f9908a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To determine which attributes and what value </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showCompartment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125529 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00517: COPY Creating style sheets

- page_id: `296125529`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296125529/COPY+Creating+style+sheets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-03-26T12:07:24.586+01:00`
- ancestors: COPY Symbol styles > COPY Custom style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create custom rule-based symbol styles by creating a style sheet. However, there are two ways you can do that:

- This option allows you to create your own custom rules as well as reuse and customize all the rules already available in the reused style sheet. The reused style sheet can be either a predefined one (e.g., *DefaultStyleSheet*) or a custom-created one. For example, you can reuse the *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet, then customize its rules by changing their names, attributes, and their values, etc. Additionally, if you create rules only for selected symbols, overwriting the DefaultStyle*Sheet* 's rules, all the other symbols you do not overwrite will continue to be represented according to the DefaultStyle*Sheet* .
- This option allows you to create your own custom rules only without reusing any predefined or custom style sheets and their rules . Choose this option if you prefer creating your own rules without the need to reuse or customize the existing ones.

##### Creating a style sheet

###### Reusing an existing style sheet

To reuse an existing style sheet

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Textual Editor' space=''] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing and filtering elements' space=''] the DS_Styles , CoreStylesComponents, Predicates , ColorDefinitions , CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions , KerMLStyles , and SysMLStyles packages. To learn more about each package, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Custom style sheets' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Custom style sheets' space=''] page.
5. Declare a part definition, specify the name for the new style sheet, subclassify the part definition of the existing style sheet you want to reuse (e.g., DefaultStyleSheet ). You can now [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Customizing style sheet components' space=''] within the body of the new part definition.

packageNewStylesPackage{privateimportDS_Styles::**;privateimportCoreStylesComponents::*; 
privateimportPredicates::*; 
privateimportColorDefinitions::*; 
privateimportCompartmentVisibilityDefinitions::*; 
privateimportKerMLStyles::*; 
privateimportSysMLStyles::*; 
partdefNewStyleSheet:>DefaultStyleSheet{ 
// specify custom rules or customize those in the *DefaultStyleSheet* style sheet here 
} 
}

###### Creating a custom style sheet from scratch

To create a custom style sheet from scratch

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Textual Editor' space=''] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing and filtering elements' space=''] the DS_Styles , CoreStylesComponents, Predicates , ColorDefinitions , CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions , KerMLStyles , and SysMLStyles packages. To learn more about each package, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Custom style sheets' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Custom style sheets' space=''] page.
5. Declare a part definition with the part def keyword, specify the name for the new style sheet, subclassify the StyleSheet part definition. You can now [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''] within the body of the new part definition.

package NewStylesPackage {privateimportDS_Styles::**;privateimportCoreStylesComponents::*; 
privateimportPredicates::*; 
privateimportColorDefinitions::*; 
privateimportCompartmentVisibilityDefinitions::*; 
privateimportKerMLStyles::*; 
privateimportSysMLStyles::*;part def NewStyleSheet :>StyleSheet{ 
 // specify custom rules here } 
}

###### Creating a custom style sheet from scratch via the Style Sheets button and activating it for a specific view

To create a custom style sheet from scratch via the Style Sheets button and activate it for a specific view

1. Open a specific view.
2. Click the Style Sheets button in the view toolbar . In the dropdown menu, click the **Create and** Apply Style Sheet command. A part definition element for the new style sheet is created, and its element symbol is displayed in the view. The style sheet is also automatically activated for this view. 
You can now [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''] within the body of the new part definition. The created style sheet is empty; it does not contain any imports necessary for the style sheet component creation. For that, either see the procedure or use qualified names to access the needed elements.

definition.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d60de0d-75bf-4088-9a69-ecbd99669c6f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span
