/>}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating a custom style sheet from scratch via the Style Sheets button and activating it for a specific view</h4><p>To create a custom style sheet from scratch via the Style Sheets button and activate it for a specific view</p><hr /><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="5baf4a3d-b016-483f-a720-6d6cf8e6410f">Open a specific view. </li><li data-uuid="875ed859-d6f0-4573-bab9-8759a46e992d">Click the<span> </span><strong>Style Sheets</strong><span> </span>button<span> in the view toolbar</span>. In the dropdown menu, click the<span> <strong>Create and </strong></span><strong>Apply Style Sheet </strong>command. <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A part definition element for the new style sheet is created, and its element symbol is displayed in the view. The style sheet is also automatically activated for this view.<br />You can now </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specify custom components]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> within the body of the new part definition.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2295aa05-59c6-4cce-b8f2-3066464287c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The created style sheet is empty; it does not contain any imports necessary for the style sheet component creation. For that, either see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a custom style sheet from scratch" /> procedure or use qualified names to access the needed elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">  </span></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304015530 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00518: COPY Creating tabular views

- page_id: `304015530`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304015530/COPY+Creating+tabular+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-05-06T16:58:41.437+02:00`
- ancestors: 
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Review the procedures for creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rendering views' space=''] tabular views. For basic view creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Views basics' space=''] page.

##### Creating tabular views

###### Creating tabular views via the Create View command

The Create View command is available in the element and element symbol shortcut menus, invoked in the Containment tree or in a view.

The Create View command invokes the View Creation dialog/submenu, which displays the following view definitions:

- Standard predefined tabular view definitions. See the table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space='']page for a full list.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Custom tabular view definitions' space='']

To create tabular views via the Create View command

1. Right-click an element or element symbol that would be the owner of the new tabular view.
2. In the shortcut menu, click **Create View**. In the submenu, click the appropriate tabular view. 
The view is created as a *view usage* element and named after its owner.

###### Creating tabular views via the Textual Editor

A rendered view is produced when a view usage is defined by a view definition that carries a renderer. For tabular views, use:

- Standard predefined tabular view definitions. See the table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space='']page for a full list.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Custom tabular view definitions' space='']

To create tabular views via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create a view.
2. Declare the view keyword, specify the element name, and then define it by the needed view definition.
3. Click the Synchronize button. view'Generic Table':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt; // new view element is defined by the 'gt' i.e., tabular view definition 'Generic Table'view'Requirements Table':DS_Views::TabularViews::rt; // new view element is defined by the 'rt' i.e., tabular view definition 'Requirements Table'

###### Creating undefinedtabular views using renderers via the Textual Editor

Instead of creating tabular views defined by tabular view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify therenderer directly for the new view.

- See the table of available renderers on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rendering views' space=''] page.

To create undefined views using renderers via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create a view.
2. Declare the view keyword, and specify the element name.
3. In the body of the view element, declare the render keyword followed by the needed renderer.
4. Click the Synchronize button. view newView{ // new view element is not defined by any view definitionrenderDS_Views::CoreViews::asTable;// the view has a renderer *asTable*, rendering it as a tabular view}

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125523 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00519: COPY Custom style sheets

- page_id: `296125523`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296125523/COPY+Custom+style+sheets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-03-26T12:07:24.349+01:00`
- ancestors: COPY Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of or in addition to using the default style sheet that specifies the default symbol styles, you can create your own custom model-based symbol styles by creating style sheets with your own sets of rules.

##### Working with style sheets

Custom model-based symbol styles are specified in a style sheet. There can be several types of style sheets:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Predefined style sheets' space=''] . The predefined style sheets are located in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets*package of the*3DS**SysML Customization*library. There are two predefined style sheets in the*DS_Styles::StyleSheets*package:
  - DefaultStyleSheet
  - BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheets' space=''] . There are two types of style sheets you can create:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheets' space=''] This option allows you to create your own custom rules as well as reuse and customize all the rules already available in the reused style sheet. The reused style sheet can be either a predefined one (e.g., *DefaultStyleSheet*) or a custom-created one. For example, you can reuse the *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet, then customize its rules by changing their names, attributes, and their values, etc. Additionally, if you create rules only for selected symbols, overwriting the DefaultStyle*Sheet* 's rules, all the other symbols you do not overwrite will continue to be represented according to the DefaultStyle*Sheet* .
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheets' space=''] This option allows you to create your own custom rules only without reusing any predefined or custom style sheets and their rules . Choose this option if you prefer creating your own rules without the need to reuse or customize the existing ones.

##### Style sheet composition

A style sheet is comprised of the following components:

- Rules - specify conditions and styles for symbols.
  - Condition - specifies to which symbols the rule applies.
  - Style - specifies the style that will be applied to the symbols that comply with the specified condition.

#### PANEL: Example of a rule

Example of a rule

partattributeRule :> rule { 
part:>> condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition { // the 'condition' component specifying a predefined condition *ByElementMetaclassCondition* that the rule applies to symbols 
refitem:>> elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::AttributeUsage.metadata, SysML::Systems::AttributeDefinition.metadata); // specifying the condition with applicable element metaclasses 
} 
view:>> style : AbstractDeepPurpleStyle; // the 'style' component specifying a predefined style *AbstractDeepPurpleStyle* found in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions*package 
}

- Style definitions . There are several types of style definitions :
  - **Primary style definitions** - high-level style definitions that specify style attributes that can be used for a rule. They are contained in the *DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles* of the *3DS SysML Customization*library.
    - Primary style definitions cannot be edited and should be used to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''].
    - For a list of primary style definitions, see the 'Primary style definitions' column in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''].
  - **Auxiliary style definitions** - lower-level style definitions that reuse (subclassify) primary style definitions, specifying selected attributes and their values of the primary style definition. Auxiliary style definitions allow you to specify concrete styles which can then be reused in multiple rules instead of specifying the style for each rule locally. There are two types of auxiliary style definitions:
    - Predefined style definitions - style definitions specified in the DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions package of the *3DS SysML Customization*library (e.g., AbstractGreenStyle , AbstractRedStyle , etc.).
      - Predefined style definitions cannot be edited, but they can be customized locally to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Customizing style sheet components' space=''].
      - For a list of predefined style definitions, see the 'Predefined style definitions' column in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''].
    - **Custom style definitions** - style definitions you create yourself.

#### PANEL: Explanation in textual notation

Explanation in textual notation

// primary style definitions *SymbolStyle*and *ShapeStyle*in the *DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles*package

viewdefSymbolStyle { 
attributelineWidth : Integerdefault1; 
attributefillColor : Colordefault"#FFF0F0F0"; 
attributeuseFillColor : Booleandefaulttrue; 
attributepenColor : Colordefault"#FF424242"; 
attributetextColor : Colordefault"#000000"; 
attributefont : Fontdefault"Roboto,0,11"; 
attributewrap : Booleandefaultfalse; 
}

viewdefShapeStyle:>SymbolStyle{ 
attributecontentFillColor:Colordefault"#FFFFFFFF"{ 
} 
attributetextVerticalPosition:TextVerticalPositionEnumdefaulttextVerticalPosition::Top{ 
} 
attributecompartmentFillColor:Colordefault"#FFF0F0F0"; 
attributecompartmentTextColor:Colordefault"#000000"; 
}

// predefined (auxiliary) style definition *AbstractDeepPurpleStyle*specified for the *attributeRule* rule (external specification) in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions*package

part attributeRule :> rule { 
 part:>> condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition { 
 ref item :>> elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::AttributeUsage.metadata, SysML::Systems::AttributeDefinition.metadata); 
 } 
 view:>> style : AbstractDeepPurpleStyle; // the predefined style definition *AbstractDeepPurpleStyle***is specified for the style component of the *attributeRule* rule (external specification) 
}

viewdefAbstractDeepPurpleStyle:>ShapeStyle{ // predefined (auxiliary) style definition *AbstractDeepPurpleStyle*in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions*packagethat subclassifies *ShapeStyle* 
attribute:>>fillColordefault"#E6DEF3"; 
attribute:>>compartmentFillColordefault"#F8F5FB"; 
attribute:>>penColordefault"#20125F"; 
}

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Libraries & samples' space='']*3DS SysML Customization*library contains the *DassaultSystemesStyles*namespace, dedicated to symbol styles' customization:

- <DS_Styles> DassaultSystemesStyles
  - CoreStylesComponents
    - Contains style sheet modeling and configuration concepts.
    - **KerMLStyles**. Contains primary style definitions that can be reused (subclassified) for style sheet rules . For a list of the primary style definitions, their ref items, and their compatibility with element symbols, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] page.
    - **SysMLStyles**. Contains compartment style view definitions for style sheet rules.
    - **Predicates**. Contains condition part definitions for style sheet rules. To learn more about reusing and modifying existing or creating custom rules using primary style definitions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Customizing style sheet components' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''] pages.
  - **StyleSheets**.
    - Contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Predefined style sheets' space='']. You can reuse and customize the predefined style sheets or create new ones from scratch. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheets' space=''] page.
    - **ColorDefinitions**. Contains color definitions for style sheet rules. You can reuse and customize the predefined style definitions. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Customizing style sheet components' space=''] page.For a list of predefined style definitions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] page.
    - **CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions**. Contains compartment visibility definitions for style sheet rules .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Instead of or in addition to using the default style sheet that specifies the default symbol styles, you can create your own custom model-based symbol styles <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">by creating style sheets with your own sets of rules. </span></p><h3>Working with style sheets</h3><p>Custom model-based symbol styles are specified in a style sheet. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">There can be several types of style sheets:</span></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Predefined style sheets" /></ac:link>. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The predefined style sheets are located in the <em style="text-align: left;">DS_Styles::StyleSheets</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>package of the<span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>3DS </em></span><em style="text-align: left;">SysML Customization</em></span><span> </span>library.</span></span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">There are two predefined style sheets in the<span> <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets</em> </span>package:</span></span><ul><li><em>DefaultStyleSheet</em></li><li><em>BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet</em></li></ul></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[User-created style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. There are two types of style sheets you can create:<ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Reusing an existing style sheet"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Reuse an existing style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">This option allows you to create your own custom rules as well as reuse and customize all the rules already available in the reused </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">style sheet. The reused style sheet can be either a predefined one (e.g., <em>DefaultStyleSheet</em>) or a custom-created one. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For example, you can reuse the <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet, then customize its </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">rules </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">by changing their names, attributes, and their values, etc. Additionally, if you create rules only for selected symbols, overwriting the<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;">DefaultStyle<em>Sheet</em></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">'s rules, all the other symbols you do not overwrite will continue to be represented according to the </span><em style="text-align: left;">DefaultStyle<em>Sheet</em></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a custom style sheet from scratch"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a custom style sheet from scratch]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />This option allows you to create your own custom rules only without reusing any predefined or custom style sheets and their rules<span>. </span>Choose this option if you prefer creating your own rules without the need to reuse or customize the existing ones. </li></ul></li></ul><h3>Style sheet composition</h3><p>A style sheet is comprised of the following components:</p><ul><li><strong>Rules </strong>- specify conditions and styles for symbols.<strong> </strong><ul><li><strong>Condition </strong>- specifies to which symbols the rule applies.</li><li><strong>Style </strong>- specifies the style that will be applied to the symbols that comply with the specified condition. </li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ddf2c046-984a-4c9a-8497-3e9a511dc208"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of a rule</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> attributeRule :&gt; rule {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the 'condition' component specifying a predefined condition <em>ByElementMetaclassCondition</em> that the rule applies to symbols</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::AttributeUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, SysML::Systems::AttributeDefinition.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">); <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the condition with applicable element metaclasses</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; style : AbstractDeepPurpleStyle; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the 'style' component specifying a predefined style <em>AbstractDeepPurpleStyle</em> found in the <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions </em>package</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><strong>Style definitions</strong>. There are several types of style definitions<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">:</span><ul><li><span><strong>Primary style definitions</strong> - high-level style definitions that specify style attributes that can be used for a rule. They are contained in the <em>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles</em> of the <em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library. </span><ul><li><span>Primary style definitions cannot be edited and should be used to <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subclassify a custom style definition (external specification)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[define the style of a specific rule (local specification)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /></span></li><li><span>For a list of primary style definitions, see the 'Primary style definitions' column in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul></li><li><span><strong>Auxiliary style definitions</strong> - lower-level style definitions that reuse (subclassify) primary style definitions, specifying selected attributes and their values of the primary style definition. Auxiliary style definitions allow you to specify concrete styles which can then be reused in multiple rules instead of specifying the style for each rule locally. There are two types of auxiliary style definitions:</span><ul><li><strong>Predefined style definitions</strong> - style definitions specified in the <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions</em> <span>package of the <em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library </span>(e.g., <em>AbstractGreenStyle</em>, <em>AbstractRedStyle</em>, etc.).<ul><li><span>Predefined style definitions cannot be edited, but they can be customized locally to <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the style attributes locally for the style of a rule"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Customizing style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[define the style of a specific rule (local specification)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span>For a list of predefined style definitions, see the 'Predefined style definitions' column in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul></li><li><span><strong>Custom style definitions</strong> - style definitions you create yourself.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e80881c-27ec-4397-9ddb-43357bfd6363"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Explanation in textual notation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// primary style definitions <em>SymbolStyle </em>and <em>ShapeStyle </em>in the <em>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles </em>package</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <u>SymbolStyle</u> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> lineWidth : Integer </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> fillColor : Color </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FFF0F0F0&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> useFillColor : Boolean </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> penColor : Color </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FF424242&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> textColor : Color </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#000000&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> font : Font </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Roboto,0,11&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> wrap : Boolean </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SymbolStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">contentFillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Color</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FFFFFFFF&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textVerticalPosition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TextVerticalPositionEnum</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textVerticalPosition::Top</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">compartmentFillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Color</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FFF0F0F0&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">compartmentTextColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Color</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#000000&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// predefined (auxiliary) style definition <em>AbstractDeepPurpleStyle </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">specified for the <em>attributeRule</em> rule (external specification) in the <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions </em>package</span></span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> attributeRule :&gt; rule {<br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition { </span><br />      <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::AttributeUsage.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>, SysML::Systems::AttributeDefinition.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">);</span><br />   }<br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; style : <u>AbstractDeepPurpleStyle</u>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// t<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">he predefined style definition <em>AbstractDeepPurpleStyle</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em> </em></span>is specified for the style component of the <em>attributeRule</em> rule (external specification)</span></span> </span></span><br />}</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AbstractDeepPurpleStyle</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// predefined (auxiliary) style definition <em>AbstractDeepPurpleStyle </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">in the <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions </em>package </span>that subclassifies <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em>ShapeStyle</em></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#E6DEF3&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">compartmentFillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#F8F5FB&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">penColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#20125F&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>3DS SysML Customization library structure</h3><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3DS_SysML_Customization_library_structure.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Libraries &amp; samples" /><ac:link-body><em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library</ac:link-body></ac:link> contains the <em>DassaultSystemesStyles </em>namespace, dedicated to symbol styles' customization:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="fbd212af-3ed1-4274-a314-94652a3ca2c4"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&lt;DS_Styles&gt; DassaultSystemesStyles</span></strong><ul><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CoreStylesComponents</span></strong><ul><li>Contains style sheet modeling and configuration<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">concepts.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>KerMLStyles</strong>.<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Contains primary style</span> definitions that can be reused (subclassified) for style sheet<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rules</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3034b41d-fff4-465a-ac38-35c0abc7bbb1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a list of the primary style definitions, their ref items, and their compatibility with element symbols, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>SysMLStyles</strong>.<span> </span></span>Contains compartment style view definitions for style sheet<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rules.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Predicates</strong>.<span> </span></span>Contains condition part <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304015594 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00520: COPY Custom tabular view definitions

- page_id: `304015594`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304015594/COPY+Custom+tabular+view+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-05-06T16:58:44.411+02:00`
- ancestors: COPY Creating tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined tabular view definitions, you can create model-based custom tabular view definitions as part of your projects.By defining custom view definitions, you can with customized columns.

##### Custom tabular view definition composition

Custom tabular view definitions differ in the columns they may have. While the tool comes with predefined tabular view definitions, you can model custom tabular view definitions with tailored columns.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of (1) predefined tabular view *Generic Table*, (2) customized *Generic Table* with the *Owned Element* columns removed, (3) custom *Drone Variant Table* with custom columns.

There are two ways you can create custom tabular view definitions:

- Reuse predefined tabular view definitions
  - Reusing a predefined tabular view definition allows the new view definition to inherit the columns defined for the reused definition. You can modify the inherited columns and add new custom ones, too.
- Create new customized tabular view definitions from scratch
  - A new view definition does not inherit any columns, allowing you to specify all the columns yourself.

Creating custom tabular view definitions with customized columns is enabled by the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''],which contains all the necessary elements and configurations allowing you to create your own tabular view definitions withcustomized columns.The library also contains the *Base Table*viewdefinition, which is especially useful as it can be used as the basis for custom tabular view definitions. The library also carries predefined tabular view definitions with predefined columns.See the.

##### Predefined tabular views' columns table

The table below lists all the columns used in the predefined tabular view definitions.

| Standard predefined tabular view definition | Columns |
| --- | --- |
| Generic table | Declared NameDeclared Short NameDocumentationQualified NameOwnerOwned Element |
| Requirements table | Declared NameReq IDDocumentation |

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

The[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Libraries & samples' space='']*3DS SysML Customization*library contains the *DassaultSystemesViews*namespace that enables the custom tabular view definition creation:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- **<DS_Views> DassaultSystemesViews**
  - **CoreViews**. Contains abstract view definitions for symbolic and tabular view definition creation; tabular view column and cell modeling concepts, their rendering definitions and usages; style sheet activation concepts.
  - **SymbolicViews**. Contains predefined symbolic views.
  - **SymbolicViewsByExpression**. Contains predefined symbolic views by expression.
  - **TabularViews**. Contains predefined tabular views.
  - **ViewPalettes**. Contains predefined view palettes .
    - **CodeActionIdentifiers**. Contains action IDs for the predefined buttons used in the symbolic view palettes of all predefined symbolic view definitions. They can also be used for custom view palettes . The predefined buttons contain names, descriptions, and actions for specific element creation.
    - **ElementTemplates**. Contains certain element templates for view palettes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125533 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00521: COPY Customizing style sheet components

- page_id: `296125533`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296125533/COPY+Customizing+style+sheet+components
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-03-26T12:07:24.816+01:00`
- ancestors: COPY Symbol styles > COPY Custom style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for customizing the style sheet components.

- The procedures on this page use the example of customizing rules of the DefaultStyleSheet style sheet, however, customization of any other style sheet's rules, whether predefined or custom, is analogous.
- For procedures on supplementing customized style sheets with your own custom rules (instead of or in addition to customizing existing ones), see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''] page.

##### Customizing style sheet components

###### Reusing style sheet**rules

To reuse style sheet rules

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheets' space='']Reuse a**style sheet (e.g., DefaultStyleSheet ).
2. Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, declare a part usage, specify a new name for the rule (optional), redefine the needed rule, and in its body, do any of the following as needed:
  1. .
  2. .
  3. .
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='']. partPortRule rule defined in the DefaultStyleSheet style sheetpackageNewStylesPackage{privateimportDS_Styles::**;privateimportCoreStylesComponents::*; 
 privateimportPredicates::*; 
 privateimportColorDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportCompartmentVisibilityDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportKerMLStyles::*; 
 privateimportSysMLStyles::*; 
partdefNewStyleSheet:>DefaultStyleSheet{partcustomizedPartPortRule:>>partPortRule{ // redefining and renaming the *partPortRule* rule 
// customize the redefined rule 
} 
} 
}

###### Modifying the style definition defining the style of a rule

To modify the style definition defining the style of a rule

1. Reuse a style sheet**rule.
2. Declare a view usage, define it by the needed auxiliary style definition, and redefine the inherited part *style*. You can define the view by multiple style definitions, listing them in a comma-separated list.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='']. partdefNewStyleSheet:>DefaultStyleSheet{ 
partcustomizedPartPortRule:>>partPortRule{ 
view:AbstractCyanStyle:>>style; // the *AbstractGreenRule*inherited from the *partPortRule*via the *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet is replaced with the predefined style definition *AbstractCyanStyle* 
} 
}

###### Modifying the styleattributes locallyfor the style of a rule

There are two ways you can specify a style for a rule:

- Local specification allows you to specify the style of a specific rule only. See the procedure .
- External specification allows you to create a custom style definition outside the rule itself, which can then be reused for the style specification of different rules. See the subsection [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''] page.

To modify the style attributes locally for the style of a rule

1. Reuse a style sheet**rule.
2. Declare a view usage, redefine the inherited part *style*, and in its body, redefine the needed attribute, specifying the new value. The attributes available to the specified style definition depend on the style definitions they subclassify. For an extensive list of the predefined style definitions and the primary style definitions they subclassify, and their available attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] page.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='']. part def NewStyleSheet:>DefaultStyleSheet{ 
partcustomizedPartPortRule:>>partPortRule{ 
view:>>style{ 
attribute :>> fillColor default "#D9EEDA"; // redefining the attribute *fillColor* available via the predefined style definition *AbstractGreenRule* 
} 
} 
}

###### Modifying thecompartment styleof a rule

The majority of the compartments are disabled (set to *false*) for most of the elements in the *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet via the *HideAllCompartments* predefined compartment style definition.

To modify the compartment style of a rule

1. Reuse a style sheet**rule.
2. Declare a view usage, redefine the inherited part *style*. In its body, declare a part for the needed compartment and define it by its definition. In its body, redefine the needed attribute, specifying the new value. To determine which primary compartment style definitions (including their attributes and value types) are available for each element symbol, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] page.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='']. partdefNewStyleSheet:>DefaultStyleSheet{ 
partnewPartPortRule:>>partPortRule{ 
view:>>style{ 
partattributeCompartment:AttributesCompartmentStyle{ 
:>>showCompartmentdefaulttrue;// the inherited *attributeCompartment* part's *showCompartment* attribute's value is changed to *true* 
} 
} 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

the redefined rule</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">    }</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b8279e06-6ee6-45f0-be4e-ce04ed0e085e">Modifying the style definition defining the style of a rule</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></h4><p>To modify the style definition defining the style of a rule</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Reusing style sheet rules"><ac:link-body>Reuse a style sheet<em> </em>rule</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a view usage, define it by the needed auxiliary style definition, and redefine the inherited part <em>style</em>.</span><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4de60b23-f19b-41d6-9619-5ce318271f0c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can define the view by multiple style definitions, listing them in a comma-separated list.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a227edf1-38cb-4b61-8f06-a45d20ea5475"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customizedPartPortRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partPortRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">the <em>AbstractGreenRule </em>inherited from the <em>partPortRule </em>via the <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet is replaced with the predefined style definition <em>AbstractCyanStyle</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4><span>Modifying the style </span><span>attributes locally </span><span>for the style of a rule</span></h4><p>There are two ways you can specify a style for a rule:</p><ul><li>Local specification allows you to specify the style of a specific rule only. See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the style attributes locally for the style of a rule" />.</li><li>External specification allows you to create a custom style definition outside the rule itself, which can then be reused for the style specification of different rules. See the subsection <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating custom style definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating style sheet components" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul><p>To modify the style attributes compartments are disabled (set to <em>false</em>) for most of the elements in the <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet via the <em>HideAllCompartments</em> predefined compartment style view usage, redefine the inherited part <em>style</em>. In its body, redefine the needed attribute, specifying the new value.</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb8c8880-e28e-45bb-9747-60e585fc5aca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To determine which primary compartment style definitions (including their attributes and value types) are available for each element symbol, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol compartment styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol compartment styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b872d7c9-2193-4f1f-b8bb-a09d36e490e0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DefaultStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newPartPortRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partPortRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">attributeCompartment </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AttributesCompartmentStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showCompartment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the inherited <em>attributeCompartment</em> part's <em>showCompartment</em> attribute's value is changed to <em>true</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><br /></span></span></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304015573 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00522: COPY Managing tabular view cells

- page_id: `304015573`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304015573/COPY+Managing+tabular+view+cells
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-05-06T16:58:43.852+02:00`
- ancestors: COPY Creating tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''] cells are the intersections of every row and column, representing the appropriate element values. Tabular view cells may contain singular or multiple values.

You can modify a table cell either directly through the cell or via the **Review and Modify**dialog; however, the availability of these functionalities depends on the feature displayed by the cell's column. For more information, see the notes in and subsections.

Tabular view cells are modeled via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Modeling tabular view columns' space=''].

##### Modifying a table cell directly via the cell

You can modify a table cell directly via the cell if it is a*[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Tabular view columns' space='']* column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values. E.g., declared name, ID, etc. It doesn't apply to cells listed in the note for the subsection.

###### Modifying a table cell value directly via the cell

To modify a table cell value directly via the cell

1. In a tabular view, double-click the cell whose value you want to modify.
2. Specify the new value.
3. Click elsewhere on the table to save the changes.

###### Modifying a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell

To modify a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell

1. In a tabular view, double-click the cell whose value you want to modify and do one of the following:
  1. Input the new value.
  2. Click the value field to display the dropdown menu with suggested applicable multiplicity values.
2. Click elsewhere on the table to save the changes. [ATTACHMENT filename='Modifying a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell.png']

##### Managing cells via the Review and Modify dialog

You can review and modify all values in the cell through the **Review and Modify**dialog.

You can modify a table cell via the**Review and Modify**dialog if it is:

- Element-type cell of a column representing the following meta-features:
  - SysML::Definition::owned<ElementKind>, e.g., SysML::Definition::ownedAction
  - SysML::Usage::nested<ElementKind>, e.g., SysML::Usage::nestedAction
  - KerML::Element::ownedElement
  - KerML::Type::ownedFeature
  - KerML::Namespace::ownedMember
- Multi-primitive-type cell of a column representing the following meta-features:
  - KerML::Element::aliasIds (multi-string)
  - KerML::Element::documentation (multi-string) - allows you to edit the owned documentation body's text, not the documentation element itself. For example, creating a new string creates a new documentation element for the rowElement with the specified documentation body.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Review and Modify dialog

To open the Review and Modify dialog

Do one of the following:

- Double-click the cell or an element in the cell. The More button appears. Click the button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Review and Modify dialog 1.png']
- In a tabular view, click the +<number> more button at the bottom of the cell. Applicable only for multi-valued cells. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Review and Modify dialog 2.png'] The Review and Modify dialog opens, displaying all values of the cell.

###### Creating new elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To create new elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. Click the Add New [ATTACHMENT filename='Review and Modify dialog Add New button.png'] button. The Create Element dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, click the needed element. The element is created in the cell at the bottom of the list.
4. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

###### Deleting elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To delete elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to delete, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements.
  1. (Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column. [ATTACHMENT filename='Review and Modify dialog select element.png']
3. Click the Delete [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
4. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

###### Renaming elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To rename elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. Click the row of the element and rename as needed.
3. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

##### Managing multi-valued cells

Tabular view cells may contain multiple values. By default, multi-valued cells display up to 10 values. You can review and modify all values in the cell through the **Review and Modify**dialog.

###### Filtering elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To filter elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. At the top of the dialog, input the filter keyword. The filter updates as you type.
  1. To reset the filter, click the Clear [IMAGE alt='' src='']**** button.
3. Click OK or Cancel .

###### Reordering elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To reorder elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to move and click the Move [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon. Drag and drop the row element to the needed position. [ATTACHMENT filename='Review and Modify dialog move element.png']
3. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

###### Modifying the value count in multi-valued cells

By default, multi-valued cells display up to 10 values. You can modify the limit via the *maxDisplayedValues* attribute specified for the *DS_Views::CoreViews::Base Table*, which is reused by all predefined tabular views.

To modify the value count in multi-valued cells

1. Within the body of a view, declare an attribute redefining the maxDisplayedValues attribute and set the default limit.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

view'Drone Structure':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
filterKerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement==false; 
attribute:>>maxDisplayedValuesdefault3;// the default value limit is set to 3 for the table's multi-value cells 
renderrendering:>>asTable{...} 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Tabular view cell errors

You may encounter the following icons and buttons in tabular view column cells:

| Icon / Button | Message | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Click to load the cell. | Cell loading was canceled after exceeding 1000 ms. Click the button to restart loading; the modeling tool will wait until the cell has finished loading.If the table is only partially loaded and its data is modified (e.g., via the Containment tree, Textual Editor, etc.) without refreshing the table afterward, the icon is displayed in the cells that were not loaded. In this case, the cell cannot be reloaded; click the Refresh button in the table toolbar to refresh the entire table. |
|  | Failed to evaluate expression. | Resolve model errors related to the column expression. The error may also appear if the expression specified for the tabular view column does not apply to the specific element. |
|  | Filtering or sorting applied on partially loaded cells. | Tabular views load only the content that is immediately visible; additional rows and cell data are loaded as you scroll. If you filter or sort partially loaded column cells, a notification icon appears in the column header.To resolve the issue, see the Tabular view loading warning box in the subsection on the page. |

data-uuid="a7993a2e-c033-4f5b-8398-8cacbfe9738f">In a tabular view, double-click the cell whose value you want to modify.</li><li data-uuid="6b131bcc-1dc1-4258-a1f9-01faf2ca9a2f">Specify the new value.</li><li data-uuid="c7cc8032-6344-4847-9c23-03f590e4e89a">Click elsewhere on the table to save the changes.</li></ol><h4>Modifying a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell</h4><p>To modify a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="17df28d0-c110-4476-ac43-868c786f97aa">In a tabular view, double-click the cell whose value you want to modify and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="68a89be3-ef45-4840-87f0-f82d1c7688ea">Input the new value.</li><li data-uuid="529de0ee-382b-43a8-8f22-00ccc10dd842">Click the value field to display the dropdown menu with suggested applicable multiplicity values.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="1b739330-1b98-43e2-a083-d8d31d485a32">Click elsewhere on the table to save the changes.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Modifying a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Managing cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h3><p>You can review and modify all values in the cell through the <strong>Review and Modify </strong>dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5435340a-1fe5-4bf2-9523-137bc90118d4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can modify a table cell via the </span><strong>Review and Modify </strong>dialog if it is:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b362761d-0899-4387-8a2f-628943d227f2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Element-type cell of a </span>column representing the following meta-features:<ul><li>SysML::Definition::owned&lt;ElementKind&gt;, e.g., SysML::Definition::ownedAction</li><li>SysML::Usage::nested&lt;ElementKind&gt;, e.g., SysML::Usage::nestedAction</li><li>KerML::Element::ownedElement</li><li>KerML::Type::ownedFeature</li><li>KerML::Namespace::ownedMember</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="1a5898a3-0ae8-4c8d-8a4a-cb04e7de32fe"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Multi-primitive-type cell </span>of a column representing the following meta-features:<ul><li>KerML::Element::aliasIds (multi-string)</li><li>KerML::Element::documentation (multi-string) - allows you to edit the owned documentation body's text, not the documentation element itself. For example, creating a new string creates a new documentation element for the <em>rowElement</em> with the specified documentation body.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To open the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="44f5af1c-80fc-4be9-9ed4-4b68dcce9b66">Double-click the cell or an element in the cell. The <strong>More</strong> button appears. Click the button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening the Review and Modify dialog 1.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="3d825b4a-ed1b-4529-aebe-23bfeabdb4b9">In a tabular view, click the <strong>+&lt;number&gt; more </strong>button at the bottom of the cell. Applicable only for multi-valued cells. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening the Review and Modify dialog 2.png" /></ac:image><br />The <strong>Review and Modify </strong>dialog opens, displaying all values of the cell.</li></ul><h4>Creating new elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To create new elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="632e46ac-efaf-47ac-b11a-eaa7bc7d6e88"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Review and Modify dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Review and Modify dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the multi-valued cell.</li><li data-uuid="11269334-32df-4a59-91d4-dab56d11d71e">Click the <strong>Add New</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog Add New button.png" /></ac:image><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span></strong>button. The <strong>Create Element </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="f2eecf93-d076-46a1-8568-8898135a7b7a">In the dialog, click the needed element. The element is created in the cell at the bottom of the list.</li><li data-uuid="e6607e7f-3544-41cc-aa80-155ad86d8d36">Click <strong>OK</strong>. Otherwise, to discard changes, click <strong>Cancel</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Deleting elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To delete elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ba49e992-4fe4-4ba8-9c83-039987b38eb9"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Review and Modify dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Review and Modify dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the multi-valued cell.</li><li data-uuid="64a6bc19-f350-4864-9763-bfe7bbab8034">In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to delete, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements.<ol><li data-uuid="d5319137-919d-4088-b905-920c90b54afb">(Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog select element.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="096eb0aa-5fe9-41aa-b6b3-8a46e6067ba1">Click the <strong>Delete <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog Delete button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button.</li><li data-uuid="1aa28012-0c54-4fd9-8ab4-b89adb560eb5">Click <strong>OK</strong>. Otherwise, to discard changes, click <strong>Cancel</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Renaming elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To rename elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="21b5f411-3986-4b48-8708-769b464edbad"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Review and Modify dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Review and Modify dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the multi-valued cell.</li><li data-uuid="a2395c6c-bfc5-4433-941d-c057f4af2aab">Click the row of the element and rename as needed.</li><li data-uuid="6f8c57d1-77b3-4452-bb23-c71690e42785">Click <strong>OK</strong>. Otherwise, to discard changes, click <strong>Cancel</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Managing multi-valued cells</h3><p>Tabular view cells may contain multiple values. By default, multi-valued cells display up to 10 values. You can review and modify all values in the cell through the <strong>Review and Modify </strong>dialog.</p><h4>Filtering elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To filter elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7bb85797-4630-4e41-821d-89fb02374387"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Review and Modify dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Review and Modify dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the multi-valued cell.</li><li data-uuid="25d1ea15-e756-4798-8848-99e9ed55b021">At the top of the dialog, input the filter keyword. The filter updates as you type.<ol><li data-uuid="17db893d-87d5-46e6-892e-91b5ed540ef5">To reset the filter, click the <strong>Clear <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog Clear button.png" /></ac:image><strong> </strong></strong>button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="ff061a26-4659-44eb-b9f7-b4fe5df65603">Click <strong>OK </strong>or <strong>Cancel</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Reordering elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To reorder elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ab171e68-87fb-41c7-b593-f0e165510d5d"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Review and Modify dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Review and Modify dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the multi-valued cell.</li><li data-uuid="c77f2758-e8be-4f3f-a9a0-0755d05ec447">In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to move and click the <strong>Move <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog move icon.png" /></ac:image></strong> icon. Drag and drop the row element to the needed position.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog move element.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="22cbb639-f078-49e0-8966-724a589a9537">Click <strong>OK</strong>. Otherwise, to discard changes, click <strong>Cancel</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Modifying the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2cc45aa1-d383-49b0-967a-edc7817035a1">value</ac:inline-comment-marker> count in multi-valued cells </h4><p>By default, multi-valued cells display up to 10 values. You can modify the limit via the <em>maxDisplayedValues</em> attribute specified for the <em>DS_Views::CoreViews::Base Table</em>, which is reused by all predefined tabular views.</p><p>To modify the value count in multi-valued cells</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="16c5123d-67af-4351-9765-9bf10c373641">Within the body of a view, declare an attribute redefining the <em>maxDisplayedValues</em> attribute and set the default limit.</li><li data-uuid="fae7c2a5-973e-420a-8839-bda4fcb20ccf">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a39e7e3-9da5-4b94-befd-a157a6df2456"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Drone Structure'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the default value limit is set to 3 for the table's multi-value cells</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span
