style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="maxDisplayedValues attribute.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Tabular view cell errors</h3><p>You may encounter the following icons and buttons in tabular view column cells:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Icon / Button</th><th scope="col">Message</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Click to load the cell icon.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to load the cell.</td><td><p>Cell loading was canceled after exceeding 1000 ms. Click the button to restart loading; the modeling tool will wait until the cell has finished loading.</p><p>If the table is only partially loaded and its data is modified (e.g., via the Containment tree, Textual Editor, etc.) without refreshing the table afterward, the icon is displayed in the cells that were not loaded. In this case, the cell cannot be reloaded; click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button in the table toolbar to refresh the entire table. </p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Failed to evaluate expression icon.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Failed to evaluate expression.</td><td>Resolve model errors related to the column expression. The error may also appear if the expression specified for the tabular view column does not apply to the specific element.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering or sorting partially loaded cells notification.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Filtering or sorting applied on partially loaded cells.</td><td><p>Tabular views load only the content that is immediately visible; additional rows and cell data are loaded as you scroll. If you filter or sort partially loaded column cells, a notification icon appears in the column header.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To resolve the issue, see the <strong>Tabular view loading </strong>warning box in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Filtering tabular view column cells"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Filtering tabular view column cells]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> subsection on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Modeling tabular view columns" /></ac:link> page.</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304015550 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00523: COPY Managing tabular view rows

- page_id: `304015550`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304015550/COPY+Managing+tabular+view+rows
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-05-06T16:58:43.313+02:00`
- ancestors: COPY Creating tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each table row appearing along the side of the table represents a model element. The procedures on this page describe how to manage elements in tabular views, such as how to add, remove, delete them, etc.

##### Adding elements to the table

It is generally more convenient to add elements to tables by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exposing elements for views' space=''] and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering elements for views' space='']. However, adding individual elements to tables by exposing them is also possible.

###### Adding elements to the table via the Textual Editor

To add elements to the table via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the tabular view's body, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exposing elements for views' space=''] the element (already created in the model) for the view.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewVehicle:DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{ 
exposevehicle; 
} 
partvehicle;

###### Adding elements to the table via drag and drop

To add elements to the table via drag and drop

- S elect element(s) in the Containment tree or element symbol(s) in a view, then drag and drop them onto the diagrammatic representation of a tabular view. 
The element is added to the table. The element position may change after reopening or reloading the table, depending on the row sorting.If the dropped elements do not comply with the filter conditions specified for the table or the elements are already added to the table, the elements are not added to the table, and the following notification is displayed.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding elements to the table via the Add New command

To add elements to the table via the Add New command

- In the tabular view's toolbar, click the Add New button, and in the dropdown menu, click the element you want to create and add to the table. The element is created in the model and displayed in the table by exposing it.

- The Add New dropdown menu complies with the filters specified for the view. If certain elements are filtered out for the view, they do not appear in the dropdown menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='Table Row Management Add New Button.png']
- By default, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] that is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activating Element Creation dialog' space=''] is used to suggest elements to be created in the Add New dropdown menu. If you want a custom table-specific [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] to be used in the specific table's (tabular view usage or definition) Add New dropdown menu instead, see the procedure.

###### Custom table-specific Add New button dropdown menu

You can create a custom table-specific [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] to be used in the specific table's (tabular view usage or definition) **Add New**dropdown menu instead of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] activated for the entire project. This way, you can control what elements are available in the **Add New** dropdown menu.

- This procedure also affects the suggested columns available for selection in the Configure Table Columns dialog. The elements you specify for the dropdown menu are also made available as the suggested columns in the Configure Table Columns dialog, including the meta-features of the specified metaclass/metadata.
- Keep in mind that if the specified elements do not satisfy the view's filters, they will not appear in the Add New dropdown menu, and the Add New button will be disabled.

To create a custom table-specific **Add New**button dropdown menu

1. Within the body of a tabular view, create a part usage that redefines the creationDialog and define it by the needed Element Creation Dialog configuration (e.g., DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog ).
2. Within the body of the part, create the needed Element Creation Dialog configuration. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Element Creation dialog' space=''] page for relevant procedures. A custom table-specific Element Creation dialog is now used in the specific table's Add New dropdown menu.

50%

view'Security Requirements':DS_Views::TabularViews::rt{ 
exposeSecurityStructure::*;

// redefines the inherited*creationDialog*and is defined by the conceptual Element Creation dialog*ElementCreationDialog*configuration: 
part:>>creationDialog:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog{

// custom Element Creation Dialog configuration: 
partsysMLElementsMenu:>itemCategories{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Security"; 
part:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{ 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationByMetaclass:>>operation{ 
inref=SysML::Systems::RequirementUsage.metadata; // specifies a reuiqirement usage 
} 
} 
part:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{ 
attribute:>>label="security requirement"; 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{ 
inref= (SecurityRequirementTemplatemetaKerML::Package).ownedElement; // specifies custom element via its template 
} 
} 
} 
} 
} 
packageSecurityRequirementTemplate{ 
#SecurityStructure::Approvalrequirement; 
} 
packageSecurityStructure{ 
privateimportScalarValues::*; 
metadatadefApproval{ 
attributeapproved:Boolean; 
attributeapprover:String; 
} 
requirement<'1'>secureCommReq{ 
doc/* All external communication shall be encrypted using AES-256. */ 
@Approval{ 
:>>approved=true; 
:>>approver="Chief Security Engineer"; 
} 
} 
requirement<'2'>dataAtRestEncryptionReq{ 
doc/* All stored mission data shall be encrypted at rest using approved cryptographic algorithms. */ 
@Approval{ 
:>>approved=true; 
:>>approver="Data Protection Officer"; 
} 
} 
requirement<'3'>intrusionDetectionReq{ 
doc/* The drone shall detect and log repeated unauthorized access attempts. */ 
@Approval{ 
:>>approved=false; 
:>>approver="Cyber Defense Team"; 
} 
} 
requirement<'4'>emergencyOverrideReq{ 
doc/* An authenticated emergency override mechanism shall allow safe mission termination. */ 
@Approval{ 
:>>approved=true; 
:>>approver="Safety & Security Board"; 
} 
} 
}

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Hiding elements from the table

Hiding elements from the table filters out the selected elements from the table view without permanently .

###### Hiding elements from the table via the Textual Editor

To hide elements from the table via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the tabular view, declare a filter with the DS_Functions::IsNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency function, passing the view you want to filter using a metadata access expression and the self keyword.
2. Declare a dependency, whose source is the view passed in the function, and the target - the elements you want to hide from the view.
3. Click the Synchronize button. Filter for removing an element from the table---------Recommended approach:// filter targets the view: 
filterDS_Functions::IsNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency('Drone Structure'.metadata,self); 
// a dependency acts as a filter, removing from the targeted view ('*Drone Structure*') the specific elements (*Drone* and *MainBody*): 
dependencyfrom'Drone Structure'toMainBody, Drone;---------Alternative (costly):filterSequenceFunctions::excludes(Drone.metadata,self); 
filterSequenceFunctions::excludes(MainBody.metadata,self);

###### Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command

To hide elements from the table via the Remove From Table command

- In a tabular view, do one of the following:
  - In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to hide, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements. In the table toolbar, click the Remove From Table button.
    - (Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting check boxes in the numbering column.png']
  - Right-click an element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click Remove From Table . [ATTACHMENT filename='Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command 2.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command 3.png'] The row element is hidden from the table. This is achieved by filtering out the selected element from the table (see the example in the procedure).

##### Deleting elements from the table

###### Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command

To delete elements from the table via the Delete command

- In a tabular view, do one of the following:
  - In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to delete, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements. In the table toolbar, click the Delete button.
    - (Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Right-click an element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click Delete . [ATTACHMENT filename='Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command 2.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command 3.png'] The row element is deleted from the table and the model.

##### Selecting table elements in the Containment tree

###### Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command

To select table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command

- In a tabular view, right-click the element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click the Select in Containment Tree command.
  - If the cell is a ColumnByFeatureView column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values, the row element is selected in the Containment tree (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command 3.png']
  - Otherwise, you can choose to select the row element or the cell value in the Containment tree (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command 2.png']

##### Opening the Specification Panel for table elements

###### Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command

To open the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command

- In a tabular view, right-click the element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click the Specification command.
  - If the cell is a ColumnByFeatureView column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values, you can open the Specification Panel for the row element (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command 3.png']
  - Otherwise, you can choose to open the Specification Panel for the row element or the cell value (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command 2.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Each table row appearing along the side of the table represents a model element. The procedures on this page describe how to manage elements in tabular views, such as how to add, remove, delete them, etc. </p><h3>Adding elements to the table</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5acb487d-80bf-4573-b054-426962f11d7b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is generally more convenient to add elements to tables by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exposing entire relevant packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and then <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[narrowing down their elements through filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. However, adding individual elements to tables by exposing them is also possible.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Adding elements to the table via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To add elements to the table via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7e9feffc-7bcc-4fe8-a9b7-a2c74e5a27b7">In the Textual Editor, within the tabular view's body, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expose]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the element (already created in the model) for the view.</li><li data-uuid="9a8c7c31-820d-4396-be20-29fda02eb33d">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3e3aa2b-7b83-43ad-9573-f1847148361a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Adding elements to the table via drag and drop</h4><p>To add elements to the table via drag and drop</p><hr /><ul><li>S<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">elect element(s) in the Containment tree or element symbol(s) in a view, then drag and drop them onto the diagrammatic representation of a tabular view. <br />The element is added to the table.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e491f91d-60b2-4b00-9e8c-55f0dc276ef4"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="69878c9f-ec2a-476d-9257-c30639f85571">The element position may change after reopening or reloading the table, depending on the row sorting.</li><li data-uuid="4578ee68-7fc8-4a4a-b99c-9d15b6480654">If the dropped elements do not comply with the filter conditions specified for the table or the elements are already added to the table, the elements are not added to the table, and the following notification is displayed.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operation canceled or partially completed.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li></ul><h4>Adding elements to the table via the Add New command</h4><p>To add elements to the table via the Add New command</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="a7fec09f-2b16-4038-8daa-61ca17e024bf">In the tabular view's toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button, and in the dropdown menu, click the element you want to create and add to the table.<br />The element is created in the model and displayed in the table by exposing it.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c41d89e-970f-4c80-bb58-75e3860b728e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="9ed768c5-5367-4589-aa98-532583729951">The <strong>Add New</strong> dropdown menu complies with the filters specified for the view. If certain elements are filtered out for the view, they do not appear in the dropdown menu.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Table Row Management Add New Button.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="49a1ce65-504c-4009-a23e-cc7ec5af8134">By default, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom Element Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Creation dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that is <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activating Element Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[activated for the project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is used to suggest elements to be created in the <strong>Add New </strong>dropdown menu. If you want a custom table-specific <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom Element Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Creation dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to be used in the specific table's (tabular view usage or definition) <strong>Add New </strong>dropdown menu instead, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Custom table-specific Add New button dropdown menu" /> procedure.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Custom table-specific Add New button dropdown menu</h4><p>You can create a custom table-specific <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom Element Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Creation dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to be used in the specific table's (tabular view usage or definition) <strong>Add New </strong>dropdown menu instead of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom Element Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Creation dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> activated for the entire project. This way, you can control what elements are available in the <strong>Add New</strong> dropdown menu.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff0011bf-b477-4194-9c32-234314c6d41f"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="e6c4f1f1-3d8f-492f-b4f5-3927c564771a">This procedure also affects the suggested columns available for selection in the <strong>Configure Table Columns </strong>dialog. The elements you specify for the dropdown menu are also made available as the suggested columns in the <strong>Configure Table Columns </strong>dialog, including the meta-features of the specified metaclass/metadata.</li><li data-uuid="5f949879-71e7-458e-9ca8-e2d854774051">Keep in mind that if the specified elements do not satisfy the view's filters, they will not appear in the <strong>Add New </strong>dropdown menu, and the <strong>Add New </strong>button will be disabled.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a custom table-specific <strong>Add New </strong>button dropdown menu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="60939a58-7a73-4ea0-98fd-0f78c9572022">Within the body of a tabular view, create a part usage that redefines the <em>creationDialog </em>and define it by the needed Element Creation Dialog configuration (e.g., <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog</em>).</li><li data-uuid="844a482d-c4e8-4c96-8418-79f7fca50039">Within the body of the part, create the needed Element Creation Dialog configuration. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Element Creation dialog" /></ac:link> page for relevant procedures.<br />A custom table-specific Element Creation dialog is now used in the specific table's <strong>Add New </strong>dropdown menu.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a5be494-7134-4477-a471-8360d6822f9b"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9c08b73-77a2-4666-893e-38f3480176a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3183b9d6-0d89-46ea-a444-cbeda6ebe82e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span Requirements'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::rt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SecurityStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">    // redefines the inherited<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;">creationDialog<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">and is defined by the conceptual Element Creation dialog </span><em style="text-align: left;">ElementCreationDialog<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">configuration:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span></p><p>        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">itemCategories</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Security&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span #</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SecurityStructure::Approval</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">approved</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Boolean</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">approver</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">String</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'1'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">secureCommReq</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">doc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* All external communication shall be encrypted using AES-256. */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        @</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Approval</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">           :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">approved</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">approver</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Chief Security Engineer&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'2'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">dataAtRestEncryptionReq</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">doc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* All stored mission data shall be encrypted at rest using approved cryptographic algorithms. */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        @</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Approval</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5d40573-a8d9-4269-9fa3-4f15aeeaebda"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Custom table-specific Add New button dropdown menu.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Hiding elements from the table</h3><p>Hiding elements from the table filters out the selected elements from the table view without permanently <ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting elements from the table"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[deleting them from the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h4>Hiding elements from the table via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To hide elements from the table via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d39dd470-f761-4c14-aef6-2b678d974221">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the tabular view, declare a filter with the <em>DS_Functions::IsNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency </em>function, passing the view you want to filter using a metadata access expression and the <em>self </em>keyword.</li><li data-uuid="ee56f3a3-ca46-41b6-95d5-d10b7c20dfc3">Declare a dependency, whose source is the view passed in the function, and the target - the elements you want to hide from the view.</li><li data-uuid="e105e7f2-92a8-469c-97f9-da3acb83accf"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d382b04-5222-456f-bf1e-92fedb084a16"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Filter for removing an element from the table</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">---------Recommended approach: </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// filter targets the view:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Functions::IsNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">(</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Drone Structure'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">self</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">);</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a dependency acts as a filter, removing from the targeted view ('<em>Drone Structure</em>') the specific elements (<em>Drone</em> and <em>MainBody</em>):</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">dependency</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">from</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Drone Structure'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> MainBody, Drone;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">---------Alternative (costly):</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SequenceFunctions::excludes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">(</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">self</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">);</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SequenceFunctions::excludes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">(</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MainBody</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">self</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">);</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command</h4><p>To hide elements from the table via the Remove From Table command</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="96fdbaf6-d0b0-4579-9655-3aea1ed22f7a">In a tabular view, do one of the following:<ul><li data-uuid="56683c77-cdbf-4c30-8f3f-0b47f115158b">In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to hide, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements. In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Remove From Table </strong>button.<ul><li data-uuid="cea998a3-304c-40b8-8a9e-f85203667cf3">(Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting check boxes in the numbering column.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="c7880c5b-f984-4bce-b7d3-be9a5be94809">Right-click an element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Remove From Table</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command 2.png" /></ac:image> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command 3.png" /></ac:image><br />The row element is hidden from the table. This is achieved by filtering out the selected element from the table (see the example in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Hiding elements from the table via the Textual Editor" /> procedure).</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Deleting elements from the table</h3><h4>Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command</h4><p>To delete elements from the table via the Delete command</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="a7c2deff-2958-4f73-9cb1-43e192fcd682">In a tabular view, do one of the following:<ul><li data-uuid="89217221-ed56-4347-b1f5-c2086f3a78d5">In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to delete, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements. In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Delete </strong>button.<ul><li data-uuid="df582fbe-a0d4-4d75-ba86-ce8d165e0df5">(Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column.<br /><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting check boxes in the numbering column.png" /></ac:image></strong></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="50e98430-fff3-4802-9180-a245f97b3dbc">Right-click an element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Delete</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command 2.png" /></ac:image> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command 3.png" /></ac:image><br />The row element is deleted from the table and the model.</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Selecting table elements in the Containment tree</h3><h4>Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command</h4><p>To select table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="249a6a48-4da8-4d8e-8d6e-641252d50fd6">In a tabular view, right-click the element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> command.<ul><li data-uuid="8fa4a825-0332-4d0d-ae52-d7d2c3b433e6">If the cell is a <em>ColumnByFeatureView</em> column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values, the row element is selected in the Containment tree (click <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying a table cell directly via the cell"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to learn more).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command 3.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="4b31f036-e374-4e03-a063-a56474d6078a">Otherwise, you can choose to select the row element or the cell value in the Containment tree (click <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing cells via the Review and Modify dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to learn more).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command 2.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Opening the Specification Panel for table elements</h3><h4>Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command</h4><p>To open the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="d62535b3-d79c-447d-a87e-b13796aa3824">In a tabular view, right-click the element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Specification</strong> command.<br /><ul><li data-uuid="b2a3eea8-089b-4822-b438-625082f3eef3">If the cell is a <em>ColumnByFeatureView</em> column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values, you can open the Specification Panel for the row element (click <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying a table cell directly via the cell"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to learn more).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command 3.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="2c66a2ee-792e-48ae-9992-0dd90095b7d9">Otherwise, you can choose to open the Specification Panel for the row element or the cell value (click <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing cells via the Review and Modify dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to learn more).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command 2.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304015537 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00524: COPY Modeling tabular view columns

- page_id: `304015537`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304015537/COPY+Modeling+tabular+view+columns
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-05-06T16:58:42.646+02:00`
- ancestors: COPY Creating tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The procedures for modeling tabular view columns described on this page can be used for modeling columns for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating custom tabular view definitions' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating tabular views' space=''].

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

The following procedure, , must be done for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating tabular views' space=''] before modeling their columns.

##### Redefining the inherited renderer for column modeling in tabular view usages

To redefine the inherited renderer for column modeling in tabular view usages

1. Ensure the following steps are done:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating tabular views' space=''] .
  2. The view has an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exposing elements for views' space=''] .
  3. (Optional) The view has [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering elements for views' space=''] .
2. Within the body of the view usage, declare the keywords*render rendering*, (optionally) specify the name for the element,**and r edefine the inherited renderer. Since all standard predefined tabular view definitions use the *DS_Views::CoreViews::asTable***renderer, redefine the inherited *asTable* renderer for any view defined by predefined tabular view definitions.For views defined by custom tabular view definitions, check what the renderer of the latter is.
3. Click the Synchronize button. Proceed to .

// ---------- view usages

// a view usage defined by a predefined tabular view definition:

view'Drone Structure Table': DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>>asTable;// *Generic Table*has the rendering *asTable* 
}

// a view usage defined by a custom tabular view definition:

view'VCCU System Behavior Table':'System Behavior Table'{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>> asNewTable1;// *'System Behavior Table'*has the rendering *asNewTable1*, which redefines the *asTable*rendering 
}

// ---------- view definitions

// a new view definition created from scratch:

viewdef'System Behavior Table':>DS_Views::CoreViews::bt{renderrenderingasNewTable1:>>asTable;}

// a new view definition created by reusing a custom-created view definition 'System Behavior Table':

viewdef'Variant Table':> 'System Behavior Table'{ 
renderrenderingasNewTable3:>> asNewTable1;// *asNewTable3*redefines the *'System Behavior Table'* view definition's *asNewTable1* rendering, which itself redefines the *Base Table*view definition's *asTable* rendering

}

#### Adding table columns

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Tabular view columns' space=''] table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Tabular view columns' space=''] page.

##### Adding table columns by feature

###### Adding table columns by feature via the Textual Editor

If you need to modify inherited columns by feature instead of creating new ones, see the procedure.

To add table columns by feature via the Textual Editor

1. Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, specify the column name, define it by *DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView*, and subset *column*.
2. Within the column view's body, declare *ref item*, redefine *columnFeature*, and specify the expression for the targeted meta-feature or user-defined feature.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

#### SECTION: Targeting user-defined features

#### COLUMN: Alternative notation

50%

#### PANEL: Alternative notation

Targeting meta-features

view'Drone Structure':DS_Views::CoreViews::bt{ // a view reusing *Base Table*, which does not have any columns to be inherited fromexposeDroneStructure::**;filterKerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement==false;renderrendering:>> asTable{

view'Declared Name':DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new columnrefitem:>> columnFeature = declaredNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature; // targets *Declared Name*}

view'Declared Short Name': DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = declaredShortNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Declared Short Name* 
} 
viewDocumentation: DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = documentationmetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Documentation* 
} 
view'Qualified Name': DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = qualifiedNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Qualified Name* 
} 
viewOwner: DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = ownermetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Owner* 
} 
view'Owned Element': DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = ownedElementmetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets for *Owned Element* 
}} 
}

#### EXPAND: Alternative notation

Alternative notation

// the following expressions are equivalent: 
refitem:>>columnFeature=declaredShortNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature; 
refitem:>>columnFeature=declaredShortName.metadata as KerML::Core::Feature;

#### COLUMN: Targeting user-defined features

50%

#### PANEL: Targeting user-defined features

Targeting user-defined features

packageSecurityStructure{ 
privateimportScalarValues::*; 
metadatadefApproval{// custom metadata definition 
attributeapproved:Boolean; 
attributeapprover:String; 
}partdefSecurityDesign{// element with applied custom metadata 
@Approval{ 
:>>approved=true; 
:>>approver="John Smith"; 
} 
} 
} 
 
view'Security Structure':DS_Views::CoreViews::bt{ 
exposeSecurityStructure::*; 
filterhastypeSysML::PartDefinition; 
renderrendering:>>asTable{ 
view:DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:>column{// new column 
refitem:>>columnFeature=declaredName.metadata;// targets *Declared Name* 
} 
view:DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:>column{// new column 
refitem:>>columnFeature=SecurityStructure::Approval::approved.metadata;// targets the value of the 'approved' attribute of the custom metadata 'Approval' 
} 
view:DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:>column{// new column 
refitem:>>columnFeature=SecurityStructure::Approval::approver.metadata;// targets the value of the 'approver' attribute of the custom metadata 'Approval' 
} 
} 
}

###### Adding table columns by feature via the Configure Table Columns dialog

Generally, only inherited columns or those that redefine inherited ones are available for selection in the **Configure Table Columns**dialog.

- If the needed column by feature is not available for selection in the Configure Table Columns dialog, you need to add it via the Textual Editor. See the procedure.
- If the needed columns by feature for a metaclass or metadata, and their meta-features, are not available for selection in the Configure Table Columns dialog, you need to add them via the Textual Editor. See the procedure.

To add table columns by feature via the Configure Table Columns dialog

1. In a tabular view's toolbar, click the Columns button, and in its dropdown menu, click Configure Table Columns . The Configure Table Columns dialog opens.
2. In the Configure Table Columns dialog, in the Column Name column, under the Element node, locate the column you want to add to the table, then select its check box in the Added To Model column.
3. Click OK . The column is added to the model and displayed in the diagrammatic representation of the tabular view. [ATTACHMENT filename='Configure Table Columns dialog_create column.png']

###### Customizing the suggested column list in the Configure Table Columns dialog

You can customize the suggested column list in the **Configure Table Columns** dialog for a specific table (tabular view usage or definition) by specifying a *featureByCondition*part. It allows you to indicate a specific metaclass or metadata to make it available in the suggested column list, including its meta-features. The meta-features are grouped under the metaclass/metadata and are available for selection.

The specified metaclass/metadata and their meta-features appear as suggested columns in the**Configure Table Columns**dialog; however, the specified metaclass/metadata is not included in the****table's **Add New** button dropdown menu. If you want to see both, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Managing tabular view rows' space=''].

To customize the suggested column list with specific metaclass/metadata and its meta-features in the **Configure Table Columns**dialog

1. Within the body of a tabular view, create a part usage, define it by DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::ByElementMetaclassCondition , and redefine featureByCondition .
2. Within the body of the part, create a ref item and redefine elementMetaclass , then set the default to the metaclasses and/or metadata you want to include in the suggested column, listing them in parentheses using metadata access expression.

view'DroneStructure':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
filterKerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement==falseandhastypeSysML::PartUsage;

// redefining the *featureByCondition*: 
part:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::ByElementMetaclassCondition:>>featureByCondition{ 
// redefining the *elementMetaclass*and specifying the needed metaclasses: 
refitem:>>elementMetaclassdefault(KerML::Element.metadata,SysML::RequirementUsage.metadata); 
}

renderrendering:>>asTable{ 
view:>>'Declared Name'; 
view:>>Owner; 
view:>>'Owned Element'[0]; 
view:>>'Declared Short Name'[0]; 
view:>>'Documentation Body'[0]; 
view:>>'Qualified Name'[0]; 
} 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Adding table columns by expression

###### Adding table columns by expression via the Textual Editor

To add table columns by expression via the Textual Editor

1. Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, specify the element name, define it by *DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView*, and subset *column*.
2. Within the column view's body, d eclarethe keywords*render rendering*, specify the name for the element, define it by the needed cell rendering definition, and redefine *DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell*. If you do not define *asTableCell* by a cell rendering definition, it is considered as defined by the *StringCellRendering*cell rendering definition by default.The available table cell rendering can be found in the *CoreViews* package.Click here to view themrenderingdefBooleanCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefElementCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefEnumCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefIntegerCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefMetaobjectCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefRealCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefStringCellRendering:> TableCellRendering;rendering def TableCellRendering { 
 calc getValue { in rowElement [0..1] : Metaobjects::Metaobject; } 
 attribute isMultiValued [0..1] : ScalarValues::Boolean; 
 }
3. Within the body of the rendering, declare a calculation and redefine *getValue*. Specify the *in* direction, redefine the *rowElement*. Specify the needed expression. See the example below.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

view'Drone Structure':>DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{// a view reusing *Generic Table*expose**DroneStructure*::**;*filterKerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement==false; 
filterhastypeSysML::Systems::PartDefinition|hastypeSysML::Systems::PartUsage; 
renderrenderingnewTable:>>asTable{// redefining the inherited table rendering 
viewWeight:DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView:>column{// new custom column *Weight* 
renderrenderingasTableCell:DS_Views::CoreViews::RealCellRendering:>>DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell{// specifying the table column's cell rendering as *Real* 
calc:>>getValue{ 
in:>>rowElement:KerML::Root::Element; 
ifgetWeight(rowElement) @KerML::Kernel::LiteralInteger? (getWeight(rowElement)asKerML::Kernel::LiteralInteger).valueelse 
(getWeight(rowElement)asKerML::Kernel::LiteralRational).value 
} 
calcgetWeight{ 
ine:KerML::Root::Element; 
(((e.ownedElementasSysML::Systems::AttributeUsage)->ControlFunctions::select{ 
inee; 
(eeasKerML::Root::Element).name=="weight" 
}asSysML::Systems::AttributeUsage).ownedRelationshipasKerML::Kernel::FeatureValue).value 
} 
} 
} 
} 
}

###### Adding table columns by expression via the Configure Table Columns dialog

To add table columns by expression via the Configure Table Columns dialog

1. In a tabular view's toolbar, click the Columns button, and in its dropdown menu, click Configure Table Columns . The Configure Table Columns dialog opens.
2. In the Configure Table Columns dialog, click the Add Custom Column button. A new custom column is added under the Custom Columns node.
3. (Optional) You can also do the following:
  1. Click the column title to rename it.
  2. Click the Delete [ATTACHMENT filename='Configure Table Columns dialog delete column button.png'] button in the Added To Model column to delete the column from the model.
  3. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Configure Table Columns dialog display column expression.png'] button in the Expression column to open the Column Expression dialog and view the expression specified for the column.
4. Click OK . A template is created in the model for custom column creation. You can specify the expression for the template via the Textual Editor. The default cell rendering definition is *StringCellRendering*. You can modify it via the textual notation, see the procedure. Custom column templateviewunnamed1:DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView:>column{ 
renderrendering:DS_Views::CoreViews::StringCellRendering:>>DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell{ 
calc:>>getValue{inrowElement; } 
} 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Modifying table columns

##### Modifying inherited table columns

The table columns that are inherited from the standard predefined tabular view definitions are indicated in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''] table.

###### Modifying inherited table columns

To modify inherited table columns

1. To modify an inherited column, redefine the needed column. In its body, modify the column as needed (e.g., hide columns, redefine attributes).
2. Click the Synchronize button.

view'Drone Structure':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>>asTable{view :>> 'Qualified Name'; // redefining an inherited column 
} 
}

##### Hiding table columns

###### Hiding table columns via the Textual Editor

To hide table columns via the Textual Editor

1. Within the body of the table renderer, declare a view, redefine the column you want to hide from the diagrammatic representation of the view, and set its multiplicity to zero. The table columns that are inherited from the standard predefined tabular view definitions are indicated in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''] table.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

// ------ a view defined by a predefined tabular view:

view'Drone Structure': DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>>asTable{ 
view:>>'Declared Short Name'[0]; // view redefines *Declared Short Name***inherited via *Generic Table,*specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to hide it 
} 
}

###### Hiding table columns via the column headers in tabular views

To hide table columns via the column headers in tabular views

- Right-click the header of the column you want to hide from a tabular view, and in the shortcut menu, click **Hide Column**. 
The table column is hidden by setting its multiplicity to zero.

###### Hiding/displaying table columns via the Columns button in tabular views

To hide/display table columns via the Columns button in tabular views

1. In the tabular view's toolbar, click the **Columns** button.
2. In the dropdown menu, click the column you want to hide from or display in the table. The displayed columns are highlighted in the dropdown menu. The table column is hidden by setting its multiplicity to zero. [ATTACHMENT filename='Columns button displayed hidden columns.png']

##### Deleting table columns

###### Deleting table columns via the Textual Editor

To delete table columns via the Textual Editor

1. Within the body of the renderer, delete the unnecessary column view and its body.
2. Click the Synchronize button. view'Drone Structure': DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{exposeDroneStructure::**;filterKerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement==false;renderrendering:>> asTable{view'Declared Name':DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// delete the unnecessary column view and its bodyrefitem:>> columnFeature = declaredNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature; **}} 
}

###### Deleting table columns via the column headers in tabular views

The **Delete Column**command is not available for inherited columns or for columns that redefine inherited ones.

To delete table columns via the column headers in tabular views

- Right-click the header of the column you want to delete from a tabular view, and in the shortcut menu, click **Delete Column**. 
The column is deleted from the model.

###### Deleting table columns via the Configure Table Columns dialog

The **Delete**button is not available for inherited columns or for columns that redefine inherited ones.

To delete table columns via the Configure Table Columns dialog

1. In a tabular view's toolbar, click the Columns button, and in its dropdown menu, click Configure Table Columns . The Configure Table Columns dialog opens.
2. In the Configure Table Columns dialog, under the Column Name node, locate the column you want to delete from the table, then click the Delete [ATTACHMENT filename='Configure Table Columns dialog delete column button.png'] button in the Added To Model column.
3. Click OK . The column is deleted from the model.

#### Modifying table column attributes

##### Modifying table column attributes

###### Modifying table column attributes via the Textual Editor

To modify table column attributes via the Textual Editor

1. Within the column view's body, redefine the needed attribute and set its value. Column attributesTable column attributes predefined in*DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnView*are:attributeisMultiline[0..1] :Boolean; // specify whether single-valued string cells should be displayed in a multiline format.attributepinning:ColumnPinning[0..1]; // allows you to pin the column to the left or right side of the table. Available values: *left/right*.attributesorting:ColumnSorting[0..1]; // allows you to sort table rows in ascending or descending order. Available values: *asc/desc*.attributesortingOrder:Integer[0..1]; // allows you to specify the sorting order priority for the columns with the *sorting*attribute. The table rows are ordered for all columns based on the order specified using this attribute, e.g., a column with *sortingOrder* set to 0 will take the highest priority.attributewidth:Integer[0..1]; // allows you to specify the column width. The integer value is interpreted in pixels. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Tabular view columns' space='']>]]>
2. Click the Synchronize button.

view'Drone Structure':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>>asTable{ 
view:>>'Declared Name'{ 
attribute:>>sortingdefaultsorting::desc;// specifying the sorting direction (sorted in descending order) 
attribute:>>sortingOrderdefault2;// specifying the sorting order priority for the column (2 - secondary priority) 
attribute:>>isMultilinedefaulttrue;// setting multiline to true to wrap a long qualified name 
attribute:>>widthdefault200;// specifying the column width 
} 
view:>>'Declared Short Name'{ 
attribute:>>sortingdefaultsorting::asc;// specifying the sorting direction (sorted in ascending order) 
attribute:>>sortingOrderdefault1;// specifying the sorting order priority for the column (1 - primary priority) 
attribute:>>pinningdefaultpinning::left;// pinning the column to the table's left side 
} 
} 
}

##### Sorting tabular rows

###### Sorting tabular rows via the Textual Editor

To sort tabular rows via the Textual Editor

- Modify the sorting attribute via the procedure.

###### Sorting tabular rows via column headers in tabular views

If the column header displays a [IMAGE alt='' src=''] notification after attempting to sort tabular rows via column headers, see the explanation and resolution in the **Tabular view loading**warning box in the subsection.

To sort tabular rows via column headers in tabular views

- In the tabular view, click the header of the column according to which you want to sort table rows. The Sorting icon is displayed in the header, indicating the current sorting direction. Click the column header to change the sorting direction:
  - [IMAGE alt='' src=''] indicates that cells in the column are sorted in ascending order. This is the default mode.
  - [IMAGE alt='' src=''] indicates that cells in the column are sorted in descending order. To sort according to multiple columns, hold Shift when clicking the column header. The table rows are sorted by modifying the sorting attribute for the selected columns.

##### Pinning table columns

###### Pinning table columns via the Textual Editor

To pin table columns via the Textual Editor

- Modify the pinning attribute via the procedure.

###### Pinning table columns via column headers in tabular views

To pin table columns via column headers in tabular views

1. In the tabular view, right-click the header of the column you want to pin.
2. In the dropdown menu, click **Pin Column**, and in its submenu, click one of the following:
  1. **Pin to the Left**
  2. **Pin to the Right**
  3. **No Pin**

The columns are pinned by modifying the *pinning*attribute for the selected columns.

##### Modifying table column width

###### Modifying table column width via the Textual Editor

To modify table column width via the Textual Editor

- Modify the width attribute via the procedure.

###### Modifying table column width via column headers in tabular views

To modify table column width via column headers in tabular views

1. In the tabular view, do one of the following:
  1. In the table header, drag the vertical grid lines separating the columns and adjust the width as needed.
  2. Right-click the header of a column and in the shortcut menu, click one of the following:
    1. Size Column to Fit to resize the column width, adjusting it to its content.
    2. Size All Columns to Fit to resize the width of all columns, adjusting them to their content.

The columns are adjusted by modifying the *width*attribute for the selected columns.

##### Filtering tabular view column cells

#### WARNING: Tabular view loading

Tabular view loading

Tabular views load only the content that is immediately visible; additional rows and cell data are loaded as you scroll. If you filter or sort partially loaded column cells, a notification icon [IMAGE alt='' src=''] appears in the column header.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To resolve this, do one of the following:

- Right-click the column header and in the shortcut menu, click the Load All Cells command.
- Scroll through the entire table to load all content.

Once the cells are fully loaded, retry to filter or sort.

Filtering does not persist and resets automatically once you reopen the tabular view.

###### Filtering tabular view column cells

To filter tabular view column cells

1. In the tabular view, right-click the header of the column whose cells you want to filter and click Filter Column in the shortcut menu.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. Expand the Filter by Values section. Do one of the following:
    1. In the Values column, select the check boxes for the values you want to display in the column.
    2. Type the value name in the input field to narrow the search, select the Apply filter on refined values check box, and select the check boxes for the values you want to display in the column.
  2. Expand the Filter by Conditions section.
    1. Select the condition from the dropdown menu and type the value you are looking for in the input field.
3. Click OK .
4. (Optional) To reset the filter, right-click the column header with the applied filter. In the shortcut menu, do one of the following:
  1. Click Reset Column Filter .
  2. Click Filter Column , then Reset . Click OK .
