style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">) @</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Kernel::LiteralInteger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ? (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getWeight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">) </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Kernel::LiteralInteger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">value</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">else</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                        (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getWeight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">) </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Kernel::LiteralRational</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">value</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getWeight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">e</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Root::Element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                    (((</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">e</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ownedElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::AttributeUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">)-&gt;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ControlFunctions::select</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ee</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                        (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ee</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Root::Element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">name</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;weight&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                    } </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::AttributeUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ownedRelationship</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Kernel::FeatureValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">value</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Adding table columns by expression via the Configure Table Columns dialog</h4><p>To add table columns by expression via the Configure Table Columns dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="95633aee-77be-4582-ae39-3605666514e3">In a tabular view's toolbar, click the <strong>Columns </strong>button, and in its dropdown menu, click <strong>Configure Table Columns</strong>. The <strong>Configure Table Columns </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="4c4276b3-f56e-48c7-849a-9f3c2bd55959">In the <strong>Configure Table Columns </strong>dialog, click the <strong>Add Custom Column </strong>button. A new custom column is added under the <strong>Custom Columns </strong>node.</li><li data-uuid="7038c356-5538-4f95-b64b-e590a2c1c6c6">(Optional) You can also do the following:<ol><li data-uuid="6274a267-c803-4c25-8805-969a28251928">Click the column title to rename it.</li><li data-uuid="a891ead9-f6a9-45bc-850b-5082919460b3">Click the <strong>Delete</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Configure Table Columns dialog delete column button.png" /></ac:image> button in the <strong>Added To Model </strong>column to delete the column from the model.</li><li data-uuid="091dc7b5-de4f-4161-89e3-2c0b0b0be5ad">Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Configure Table Columns dialog display column expression.png" /></ac:image> button in the <strong>Expression </strong>column to open the <strong>Column Expression </strong>dialog and view the expression specified for the column.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="64db0083-1423-43e9-b838-822570b0cf09">Click <strong>OK</strong>. <br />A template is created in the model for custom column creation. You can specify the expression for the template via the Textual Editor.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="837013e3-0319-450e-89be-dba4ca370f5f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default cell rendering definition is <em>StringCellRendering</em>. You can modify it via the textual notation, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by expression via the Textual Editor" /> procedure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="685233f4-5c82-4b15-908f-18fefb6f2270"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Custom column template</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">unnamed1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">column</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::StringCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Configure Table Columns dialog_create custom column.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Modifying table columns</h2><h3>Modifying inherited table columns</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69a869db-c56f-47bc-9fb5-dd9e448a09bf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The table columns that are inherited from the standard predefined tabular view definitions are indicated in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Tabular views"><ri:page ri:content-title="Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Modifying inherited table columns</h4><p>To modify inherited table columns</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a79d17ec-650a-4ba6-90cd-885d87194a27"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To modify an inherited column, redefine the needed column. In its body, modify the column as needed (e.g., hide columns, redefine attributes).</span></li><li data-uuid="8d35d206-1d37-4b46-82f8-87f452483768"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="726cb49a-0f35-4ef7-bcda-6ec32728347e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view </span>'Drone Structure'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br />        <u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Qualified Name'</span>;</u>  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// redefining an inherited column </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Hiding table columns</span></h3><h4><span>Hiding table columns via the Textual Editor</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To hide table columns via the Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="96cece8e-e2c3-4d09-aa39-2580a298ae76"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the table renderer, declare a view, redefine the column you want to hide from the diagrammatic representation of the view, and set its multiplicity to zero.</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7774048-f217-4a1f-8250-d79c2a561b6c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The table columns that are inherited from the standard predefined tabular view definitions are indicated in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Tabular views"><ri:page ri:content-title="Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="b4918bca-3949-478b-b7fe-e45539c507d5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0da79d01-6c4d-4e59-91f4-1ca0e1f8119b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// ------ a view defined by a predefined tabular view:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Drone Structure' </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::</span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Short Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><u>];</u> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view redefines <em>Declared Short Name</em><em> </em>inherited via <em>Generic Table, </em>specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to hide it</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span>Hiding table columns via the column headers in tabular views</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To hide table columns via the column headers in tabular views</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Right-click the header of the column you want to hide from a tabular view, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Hide Column</strong>.<br />The table column is hidden by setting its multiplicity to zero.</span></li></ul><h4><span>Hiding/displaying table columns via the Columns button in tabular views</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To hide/display table columns via the Columns button in tabular views</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="485f7c8d-7ce6-40c8-961c-9a818e93c44a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the tabular view's toolbar, click the <strong>Columns</strong> button.</span></li><li data-uuid="03f4d5ce-096f-4f12-a3d9-2ce808fd30aa"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the dropdown menu, click the column you want to hide from or display in the table.<br /></span>The displayed columns are highlighted in the dropdown menu. The table column is hidden by setting its multiplicity to zero.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Columns button displayed hidden columns.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3><span>Deleting table columns</span></h3><h4>Deleting table columns via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To delete table columns via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fdb5c0e4-22d4-41c9-990d-4a1fec70e316"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the renderer, delete the unnecessary column view and its body.</span></li><li data-uuid="55457d8f-1d6e-4330-a4c1-05ae224273b3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe44fb68-4c73-467e-bd0c-e97805757787"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Drone Structure'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">      <u>view</u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><u>:&gt; column</u> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// delete the unnecessary column view and its body<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = declaredName </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em><br /></em></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4><span>Deleting table columns via the column headers in tabular views</span></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64dff24f-bce9-4a5e-9b2c-7115f796d5d1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Delete Column </strong>command is not available for inherited columns or for columns that redefine inherited ones.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To delete table columns via the column headers in tabular views</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="78fb0c8c-111f-40e7-b953-0ea49e615111"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Right-click the header of the column you want to delete from a tabular view, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Delete Column</strong>.<br />The column is deleted from the model.</span></li></ul><h4>Deleting table columns via the Configure Table Columns dialog</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7715cfb-aaba-4da4-a1d5-12fb31f1e1ca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Delete </strong>button is not available for inherited columns or for columns that redefine inherited ones.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To delete table columns via the Configure Table Columns dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="42b9baa6-445f-43bb-827f-95a5ad5d4d6b">In a tabular view's toolbar, click the <strong>Columns </strong>button, and in its dropdown menu, click <strong>Configure Table Columns</strong>. The <strong>Configure Table Columns </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="17089a70-5b09-4558-b292-15f9782e493c">In the <strong>Configure Table Columns </strong>dialog, under the <strong>Column Name </strong>node, locate the column you want to delete from the table, then click the <strong>Delete</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Configure Table Columns dialog delete column button.png" /></ac:image> button in the <strong>Added To Model </strong>column.</li><li data-uuid="f9f89a12-d623-4817-8a86-c5e997072a24">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The column is deleted from the model.</li></ol><h2>Modifying table column attributes</h2><h3>Modifying table column attributes</h3><h4>Modifying table column attributes via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To modify table column attributes via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6135872c-8ce8-44c0-8e27-bd8961390fcb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the column view's body, redefine the needed attribute and set its value.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3b7ad8d-1fd7-4b30-ad3a-be3609ff40bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Column attributes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: left;">Table column attributes predefined in<span> </span><em>DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnView </em>are:</p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMultiline</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">] : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Boolean</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specify whether single-valued string cells should be displayed in a multiline format.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">pinning</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColumnPinning</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// allows you to pin the column to the left or right side of the table. Available values: <em>left/right</em>.</span>  </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sorting</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColumnSorting</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];  <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// allows you to sort table rows in ascending or descending order. Available values: <em>asc/desc</em>.</span> </span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sortingOrder</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Integer</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">]; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// allows you to specify the sorting order priority for the columns with the <em>sorting </em>attribute. The table rows are ordered for all columns based on the order specified using this attribute, e.g., a column with <em>sortingOrder</em> set to 0 will take the highest priority.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">width</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Integer</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">]; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// allows you to specify the column width. The integer value is interpreted in pixels.</span></span></li></ul><br /><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Column kinds"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="a3c99abd-9834-43f3-83c8-99c0e91cb269"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c36c07e1-96b1-4022-96b4-7659aaf2a599"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Drone Structure'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sorting</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sorting::desc</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the sorting direction (sorted in descending order)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sortingOrder</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the sorting order priority for the column (2 - secondary priority)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMultiline</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u>   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// setting multiline to true to wrap a long qualified name</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">width</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">200</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the column width</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Short Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sorting</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sorting::asc</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the sorting direction (sorted in ascending order)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sortingOrder</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the sorting order priority for the column (1 - primary priority)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">pinning</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">pinning::left</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>;</u> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// pinning the column to the table's left side</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }    </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Sorting tabular rows</h3><h4>Sorting tabular rows via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To sort tabular rows via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="392158ce-5470-4486-a373-d4ec67c6dac1">Modify the <em>sorting</em> attribute via the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying table column attributes via the Textual Editor" /> procedure.</li></ul><h4>Sorting tabular rows via column headers in tabular views </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b35e4778-b8f6-4618-acd7-000c552e7dbf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the column header displays a <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering or sorting partially loaded cells notification.png" /></ac:image> notification after attempting to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">sort tabular rows via column headers, see the explanation and resolution in the <strong>Tabular view loading </strong>warning box in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Filtering tabular view column cells" /> subsection.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To sort tabular rows via column headers in tabular views</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="a92ea1fb-cc14-4428-8d25-4c26e5b93617">In the tabular view, click the header of the column according to which you want to sort table rows. The <strong>Sorting</strong> icon is displayed in the header, indicating the current sorting direction. Click the column header to change the sorting direction:<ul><li data-uuid="3ba11ebb-032c-4d46-b6e7-9ffa04dec96c"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sorting_1.png" /></ac:image> indicates that cells in the column are sorted in ascending order. This is the default mode.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="8cbb771a-38a6-42ae-968d-3e76e970f4e2"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sorting_2.png" /></ac:image> indicates that cells in the column are sorted in descending order.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56b6ea12-a54a-4df5-8760-31f3850dfaab"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To sort according to multiple columns, hold Shift when clicking the column header.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>The table rows are sorted by modifying the <em>sorting </em>attribute for the selected columns.</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Pinning table columns</h3><h4>Pinning table columns via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To pin table columns via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="0d04da76-f258-4b6f-9ee2-7e264f6d6f9f">Modify the <em>pinning</em> attribute via the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying table column attributes via the Textual Editor" /> procedure.</li></ul><h4>Pinning table columns via column headers in tabular views </h4><p>To pin table columns via column headers in tabular views </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="313116b6-c0fc-4463-9191-ac762540833a">In the tabular view, right-click the header of the column you want to pin.</li><li data-uuid="972c8e56-d309-48c3-b6e4-a9e1800ef941"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the dropdown menu, click <strong>Pin Column</strong>, and in its submenu, click one of the following:</span><ol><li data-uuid="071a0baf-ca49-4e2c-8569-da47933827d3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Pin to the Left</strong></span></li><li data-uuid="ef7b72c2-6403-47b7-98e6-38bf895f70e1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Pin to the Right</strong></span></li><li data-uuid="590964f3-807a-4496-8ad7-60fccb63bdd1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>No Pin</strong></span></li></ol></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The columns are pinned by modifying the <em>pinning </em>attribute for the selected columns.</span></p><h3>Modifying table column width</h3><h4>Modifying table column width via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To modify table column width via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="3a1acdd3-bccd-4025-85ee-b235a0bc77ad">Modify the <em>width</em> attribute via the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying table column attributes via the Textual Editor" /> procedure.</li></ul><h4>Modifying table column width via column headers in tabular views </h4><p>To modify table column width via column headers in tabular views </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="135cc41d-1fe1-41a7-9a11-a64a3d2e8f8d">In the tabular view, do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="a691bce1-75b1-478e-a768-167da04942b6">In the table header, drag the vertical grid lines separating the columns and adjust the width as needed.</li><li data-uuid="59b25b42-f061-4aa6-ba31-c9c1a0fd0427">Right-click the header of a column and in the shortcut menu, click one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="4fbb3863-b129-4b5d-9b04-1948adedf046"><strong>Size Column to Fit </strong>to resize the column width, adjusting it to its content.</li><li data-uuid="f00a308d-d2b5-43b7-8d21-fe16055af64f"><strong>Size All Columns to Fit </strong>to resize the width of all columns, adjusting them to their content.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The columns are adjusted by modifying the <em>width </em>attribute for the selected columns.</span></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Filtering tabular view column cells</span></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72ed2037-cbfc-48ec-be3f-9ec8c2d52b6a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tabular view loading</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Tabular views load only the content that is immediately visible; additional rows and cell data are loaded as you scroll. If you filter or sort partially loaded column cells, a notification icon <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering or sorting partially loaded cells notification.png" /></ac:image> appears in the column header.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering or sorting partially loaded cells.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To resolve this, do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="86bb4d2d-b9b1-432e-adab-e6a191345bf4">Right-click the column header and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Load All Cells </strong>command.</li><li data-uuid="48fd03c3-8ddb-4d2c-82cb-8358d92c05d7">Scroll through the entire table to load all content.</li></ul><p>Once the cells are fully loaded, retry to filter or sort.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0cbc27c4-183e-4b89-b2c8-5ae7114bcf4b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Filtering does not persist and resets automatically once you reopen the tabular view.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Filtering tabular view column cells</span></h4><p>To filter <span>tabular view column cells</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3ddb0343-ee20-47ff-ac7a-7044bb54801b">In the tabular view, right-click the header of the<strong> </strong>column whose cells you want to filter and click <strong>Filter Column</strong> in the shortcut menu.</li><li data-uuid="83dbf5d3-6134-4e9a-b633-72297ead9855">Do one of the following:<ol><li>Expand the <strong>Filter by Values</strong> section. Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the <strong>Values</strong> column, select the check boxes for the values you want to display in the column.</li><li>Type the value name in the input field to narrow the search, select the <strong>Apply filter on refined values</strong> check box, and select the check boxes for the values you want to display in the column.</li></ol></li><li>Expand the <strong>Filter by Conditions</strong> section.<ol><li>Select the condition from the dropdown menu and type the value you are looking for in the input field. </li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="e84a96e9-5205-440a-af82-4be444c94086">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="8ed36fa7-96ae-4349-8848-c412eb88e588">(Optional) To reset the filter, right-click the column header with the applied filter. In the shortcut menu, do one of the following:<ol><li>Click <strong>Reset Column Filter</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Filter Column</strong>, then <strong>Reset</strong>. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125581 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00525: COPY Modifying styles of individual symbols

- page_id: `296125581`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296125581/COPY+Modifying+styles+of+individual+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-03-26T12:07:27.610+01:00`
- ancestors: COPY Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

You can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify style properties for specific symbols, determining their appearance.

Changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Activating style sheets' space='']. To reset, see the procedure.

##### Opening the Symbol Style dialog

To open the Symbol Style dialog

Do one of the following:

- Right-click the selected element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click **Symbol Style**. To invoke the Symbol Style dialog for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Docked symbols' space=''], you must right-click the symbol's header.
- Select the element symbol(s) and press Alt+Enter .

##### Modifying individual symbol styles

To modify individual symbol styles

1. Select the symbol(s) whose styles you want to modify and .
2. Locate the property you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.
3. Click Close .

##### Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog

While changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.

To reset changes made in the Symbol Style dialog

1. .
2. Locate the property you want to reset to the style of the currently activated style sheet. Its check box in the Default column is deselected. [ATTACHMENT filename='Resetting_changes.png']
3. Select the check box in the Default column for the needed property.
4. Click Close .

##### The Symbol Style dialog

The table below lists the style properties available for modification and their descriptions.

- Modifying a symbol style changes its Default value to false .
- You can hide unwanted symbol styles from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating style sheet components' space=''] page.

In the **Symbol Style** dialog, symbol styles are ordered into the following groups:

- **Main**: this property group displays symbol styles available to the majority of element kinds.
- **Compartment-based properties**: the availability of specific symbol style groups depends on the element kind, as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='']. Some of the symbol styles available to individual compartment-based symbol style groups may be the same as in the Main group, while others are compartment-specific.
  - Compartment-based style groups can be: Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, Stakeholders, Include Use Cases, Parts, Ports, Parameters, Attributes, Ends, Satisfy Requirements , Items, Constraints, Require Constraints, Assume Constraints, Assert Constraints, Actions, Perform Actions, Exhibit States, Frames, Enumerations, and Metadata.

| Name | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main |  |  |
| Line Width | Positive number | Specify the width of the symbol's outline. |
| Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's header.To enable a gradient for this property, enable the view style property. |
| Use Fill Color | true/false | Specify whether to use a specific color for the symbol's header. |
| Pen Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's outline. |
| Text Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's text. |
| Font | Multiple font optionsMultiple style options (italic, bold, etc.)Multiple size options | Specify the font style of the symbol's text. |
| Wrap | true/false | Specify whether the text should wrap to fit the width of the element symbol. |
| Compartment Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's compartments. |
| Compartment Text Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color for the text in the symbol's compartments. |
| Show Short Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Modifiers | true/false | Specify whether the element's (e.g., in, ref, abstract, etc.) should be displayed on the element symbol. The property does not control the individual and portion kind (timeslice, snapshot) modifiers. |
| Show Full Text | true/false | Specify whether element values should be displayed in full or abbreviated. Modifying this property affects the values displayed in both the selected symbol's name and its compartments.Modifying this property does not affect element values displayed in the metadata compartment. Values in this compartment are always displayed in full.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Content Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's body.To enable a gradient for this property, enable the view style property.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Text Vertical Position | TopCenterBottom | Specify the element name position for the symbol.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.The property is only effective for element symbols displayed without their compartments. |
| Show Specialization | true/false | Specify whether the textual symbols should be displayed in the element name (e.g., the textual symbol :> or the keyword specializes, in the case of subclassification). The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Qualified Name for Specialization | true/false | Specify whether the specialization's full should be displayed in the element name.Enabling the property also requires the 'Show Specialization' property to be enabled.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Orientation | HorizontalVertical | Specify the orientation for the symbol.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Path Style | RectilinearObliqueBezier | Specify the style of the path.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Value | true/false | Specify whether the element's defined value should be displayed.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Inherited Sign | true/false | Specify whether the inherited sign should be displayed on the symbol.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Payload | true/false | Specify whether the payload name and sign should be displayed on the connector path.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Text Horizontal Position | LeftCenterRight | Specify the text position for the vertical and horizontal separator symbols. |
| Line Style | SolidDashedDottedetc. | Specify the line style for the vertical and horizontal separator symbols. |
| Is Rounded | true/false | Specify whether the rectangular shape symbol's corners should be rounded. |
| Compartment-based symbol styles (in compartment-based symbol style groups, e.g., Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, etc.) |  |  |
| Show Compartment | true/false | Specify whether the compartment should be displayed or suppressed. |
| Show Inherited | true/false | Specify whether the inherited elements should be displayed on the symbol. |
| Show Only Owned | true/false | Specify whether only the owned elements or imported ones, too, should be displayed in the symbol compartment.The property is only available for the package symbol and applies to its Members compartment. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125586 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00526: COPY Modifying styles of individual views

- page_id: `296125586`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296125586/COPY+Modifying+styles+of+individual+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-03-26T12:07:27.920+01:00`
- ancestors: COPY Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The primary ways to customize the styles of individual views:

- Activating style sheets for a specific view.
- Managing style properties for a specific view via the View Style dialog.

Changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Activating style sheets' space='']. To reset, see the procedure.

##### Activating style sheets for a specific view

- For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Activating style sheets' space=''] procedures on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Activating style sheets' space=''] page.

##### Managing style properties for a specific view via the View Style dialog

###### Opening the View Style dialog

To open the View Style dialog

- Right-click the selected view. In the shortcut menu, click **View Style**.

###### Modifying an individual view's style

To modify an individual view's style

1. Select the view(s) whose styles you want to modify and .
2. Locate the property you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.
3. Click Close .

###### Resetting changes made in the View Style dialog

While changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.

To reset changes made in the View Style dialog

1. .
2. Locate the property you want to reset to the style of the currently activated style sheet. Its check box in the Default column is deselected.
3. Select the check box in the Default column for the needed property.
4. Click Close .

###### The View Style dialog

The table below lists the style properties available for modification and their descriptions.

Modifying a view style property changes its **Default**value to *false*.

Currently, other view style properties displayed in the dialog besides the **Use Gradient Fill**are not functional.

| Name | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main |  |  |
| Use Gradient Fill | true/false | Specify whether a gradient should be used for the Fill Color and Content Fill Color . |
| Show Line Jumps On | NoneHorizontal LineVertical Line | Specify whether line jumps should be used for intersecting lines (e.g., paths).None - line jumps are not used.Horizontal Line - line jumps are used only on horizontal lines (default).Vertical Line - line jumps are used only on vertical lines. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576490 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00527: Copy of Expose - su filtered import (neveikia)

- page_id: `249576490`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576490/Copy+of+Expose+-+su+filtered+import+neveikia
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-02-25T18:13:37.434+01:00`
- ancestors: 
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

A view usage exposes a portion of a model, specifying what model elements can be displayed by the view using the relationship. The exposed elements can be further specified using . Once the elements are exposed,the view [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Renderings' space=''] can be applied to the exposed elements that meet all the view conditions to produce a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with views' space=''].

##### Element exposition kinds and visibility

Expose relationship is a kind of import relationship and is thus declared like an import relationship, but using the keyword *expose* instead of *import*. However, unlike imports, whose visibility can be modified, expose relationships always have *protected* visibility, which means that the elements exposed by a view usage are not publicly visible outside the view usage.

The exposition kind is specified as a specific suffix added to the exposed elements qualified name.

| Exposition kind | Suffix | Exposes | Excludes | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Membership exposition | none | Only the specified namespace (i.e., element import). | The namespace's elements and their sub-namespaces (elements/packages). | part employeeInformation { part candidateData {} part employeeData { item employeeName; }}view 'Employee Information' {// membership exposition: expose employeeInformation; // exposes employeeInformation} |
| Namespace exposition | ::* | The content of the specified namespace. | The namespace itself.The namespace's sub-packages and their elements. Deeper nested sub-packages/elements can be accessed via name resolution.The sub-elements of the namespace's elements. Deeper nested sub-elements can be accessed via name resolution. | part employeeInformation { part candidateData {} part employeeData { item employeeName; }}view 'Employee Information' {// namespace exposition: expose employeeInformation::*; // exposes candidateData, employeeData} |
| Recursive membership exposition | ::** | The specified namespace and its content, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | Memberships inherited via implicit specializations. | part employeeInformation { part candidateData {} part employeeData { item employeeName; }}view 'Employee Information' {// recursive membership exposition: expose employeeInformation::**; // exposes employeeInformation, candidateData, employeeData, employeeName} |
| Recursive namespace exposition | ::*::** | The content of the specified namespace, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | The namespace itself.Memberships inherited via implicit specializations. | part employeeInformation { part candidateData {} part employeeData { item employeeName; }}view 'Employee Information' {// recursive namespace exposition: expose employeeInformation::*::**; // exposes candidateData, employeeData, employeeName} |

##### Element exposition

###### Exposing elements

To expose elements

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the view element for which you want to expose the element(s).
2. Declare the keyword expose .
3. Specify the qualified name of the membership/namespace you want to import, followed by the .
4. (Optional) .
5. Click the Synchronize button.

partemployeeInformation{partcandidateData{}partemployeeData{itememployeeName;}}

view'Employee Information'{ 
expose employeeInformation::**; // recursive membershipexposition of *employeeInformation, candidateData, employeeData, employeeName*}

##### Element exposition filtering

Views have the capability to filter elements upon exposition based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions are exposed for the view.

Ways to perform element exposition filtering:

- Filtered exposition combines filter conditions with the expose relationship itself by adding filter conditions in the *expose* declaration using square brackets [...] . For a filtered exposition, memberships are exposed from that specific exposition only if they satisfy all the given filter conditions.
  - It can be used for any namespace.
- Separately declared filter conditions are declared after the expose declaration, using the keyword filter .
  - Separately declared filter conditions can be used only for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing and filtering elements' space='']) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Views' space=''] (element exposition) elements.
- Defining a view by a view definition containing filtered exposition.

- Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. It can reference only literals, metadata annotations, and attributes, connected using basic arithmetic, comparison and Boolean operators.
- @ , istype , and hastype classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.
- Element metaclass metadatas are located in used projects at Standard Libraries > SysML > Systems . You can either import the library or the needed element, or use a qualified name to target the specific element metaclass.
- Unlike separately declared filter conditions that require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., *@MetadataDefinitions::Safety*), filtered exposition declaration can discard qualified names for metadata as long as all the needed namespaces are imported.

###### Filtered exposition

To filter exposition

1. In the Textual Editor, place square brackets at the end of the *expose* declaration you want to filter.
2. Within the square brackets, specify the filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the name of the element you want to expose.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

#### PANEL: Filtered exposition

Filtered exposition

packageMetadataDefinitions{

metadatadefSafety{attributeisMandatory:Boolean;}}

packageVehicle{ 
privateimportMetadataDefinitions::**; 
partvehicle{ 
partinterior{ 
partalarm; 
partseatBelt[2] { 
@Safety{isMandatory=true;} 
} 
partfrontSeat[2]; 
partdriverAirBag{ 
@Safety{isMandatory=false;} 
} 
} 
} 
}

// no need for qualified names for metadata when using filtered exposition declaration as long as all the needed namespaces are exposed 
 
view'Vehicle Features'{ 
// no need for qualified names for metadata when using filtered exposition declaration as long as all the needed namespaces are exposed: 
exposeSysML::Systems::**;// expose metaclass metadatas 
exposeMetadataDefinitions::**;// expose metadata definitions 
 
// you can use multiple filters in the exposition, but using separate brackets: 
exposeVehicle::**[@PartUsage][@SafetyandSafety::isMandatory];// exposes part usage elements from package *Vehicle*with *Safety* metadata attribute *isMandatory*value *true* 
}

###### Separately declared filter conditions

To specify separately declared filter conditions

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the view containing an expose declaration.
2. Declare the keyword filter followed by filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the qualified name of the element you want to expose .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

#### PANEL: Separately declared filter conditions

Separately declared filter conditions

packageMetadataDefinitions{

metadatadefSafety{attributeisMandatory:Boolean;}}

packageVehicle{ 
privateimportMetadataDefinitions::**; 
partvehicle{ 
partinterior{ 
partalarm; 
partseatBelt[2] { 
@Safety{isMandatory=true;} 
} 
partfrontSeat[2]; 
partdriverAirBag{ 
@Safety{isMandatory=false;} 
} 
} 
} 
}

// separately declared filter conditions require a qualified name for metadata (e.g., 'MetadataDefinitions::Safety'), unlike filter import declaration

view'Vehicle Features'{ 
exposeVehicle::**;// exposes package *Vehicle*separately 
// you can use multiple filter condition declarations: 
filter@SysML::Systems::PartUsage;// exposes part usage elements from package *Vehicle* 
filter@MetadataDefinitions::SafetyandMetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory;// exposes elements with *Safety* metadata attribute *isMandatory*value *true*from previously filtered part usage elements exposed from the package *Vehicle* 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p> <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">On this page:</strong></p><p><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b02d6d8f-e1e2-4559-9cab-1706ee062fb3" /></span></p><hr /><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A view usage exposes a portion of a model, specifying what model elements can be displayed by the view using the <ac:link ac:anchor="Element exposition"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expose]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> relationship. The exposed elements can be further specified using <ac:link ac:anchor="Element exposition filtering"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Once the elements are exposed, </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">the view <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Renderings" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can be applied to the exposed elements that meet all the view conditions to produce a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendered view artifact]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Element exposition kinds and visibility</h3><p>Expose relationship is a kind of import relationship and is thus declared like an import relationship, but using the keyword <em>expose</em> instead of <em>import</em>. However, unlike imports, whose visibility can be modified, expose relationships always have <em>protected</em> visibility, which means that the elements exposed by a view usage are not publicly visible outside the view usage.</p><p>The exposition kind is specified as a specific suffix added to the exposed elements qualified name.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 89.7357%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 12.4917%;" /><col style="width: 4.21067%;" /><col style="width: 19.2287%;" /><col style="width: 31.0186%;" /><col style="width: 33.0425%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Exposition kind</th><th scope="col">Suffix</th><th scope="col">Exposes</th><th scope="col">Excludes</th><th scope="col">Example</th></tr><tr><td><p><span><strong>Membership exposition</strong></span></p></td><td>none</td><td><span style="color: rgb(68,71,70);">Only the specified namespace (i.e., element import).</span></td><td><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(68,71,70);">The namespace's elements and their sub-namespaces (elements/packages).</span></li></ul></td><td><div rgb(0,17,136);">candidateData</span> {}</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">    </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">part</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">employeeData</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> {</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">        </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">item</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">employeeName</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">;</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">    }</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">}</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">view</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,128,128);">'Employee Information'</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> {<br /><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">// recursive namespace exposition:</span></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">    </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">expose <span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">employeeInformation::*::**</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">;         <br /><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">// </span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">exposes <em>candidateData, employeeData, employeeName</em></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Element exposition</h3><h4>Exposing elements</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(68,71,70);">To expose elements</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the view element for which you want to expose the element(s).</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Declare the keyword </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">expose</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Specify the <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">qualified name of the membership/namespace you want to import, followed by the <ac:link ac:anchor="Element exposition kinds and visibility"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[appropriate suffix for a recursive/membership/namespace exposition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">(Optional) </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Element exposition filtering"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify a filter for the exposition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2dae463f-236c-45dd-9883-ecc7044ea2d8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">part</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">employeeInformation</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> {<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">    </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">part</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">candidateData</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> {}<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">    </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">part</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">employeeData</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> {<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">        </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">item</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">employeeName</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">;<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">    }<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">}</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">view</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,128,128);">'Employee Information'</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> {</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">    </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">expose <span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">employeeInformation::**</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">;   <span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">// recursive membership </span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">exposition of <em>employeeInformation, candidateData, employeeData, employeeName</em></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Element exposition filtering</h3><p>Views have the capability to filter elements upon exposition based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions are exposed for the view. </p><p>Ways to perform element exposition filtering:</p><ul><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Filtered exposition</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> combines filter conditions with the expose relationship itself by adding filter conditions in the <em>expose</em> declaration using square brackets </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">[...]</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. For a filtered exposition, memberships are exposed from that specific exposition only if they satisfy all the given filter conditions.</span><ul><li>It can be used for any namespace.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Separately declared filter conditions</strong> are declared after the <em>expose</em> declaration, using the keyword <em>filter</em>.<ul><li><span>Separately declared filter conditions can be used only for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing and filtering elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (element exposition) elements.<br /></span></li></ul></li><li style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);">Defining a view by a view definition containing filtered exposition.</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e981b38-7a7e-4fd8-9868-448d2c5db34b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. It can reference only literals, metadata annotations, and attributes, connected using basic arithmetic, comparison and Boolean operators.</li><li><em>@</em>, <em>istype</em>, and <em>hastype</em> classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.</li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Element metaclass metadatas are located in used projects at </span><em style="text-align: left;">Standard Libraries &gt; SysML &gt; Systems</em><em style="text-align: left;">. </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can either import the library or the needed element, or use a qualified name to target the specific element metaclass. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Unlike separately declared filter conditions that require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., <em>@MetadataDefinitions::Safety</em>), </span>filtered exposition declaration can discard qualified names for metadata as long as all the needed namespaces are imported.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Filtered exposition</h4><p>To filter exposition</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">In the Textual Editor, place square brackets at the end of the <em>expose</em> declaration you want to filter.</span></li><li>Within the square brackets, specify the filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the name of the element you want to expose.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9e8499b-4a1a-4167-af80-da639ceb9515"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Filtered exposition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">package</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,128,128);">MetadataDefinitions</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">    </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">metadata</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">def</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,128,128);">Safety</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> {<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">        </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">attribute</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">isMandatory</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> : </span><span style="color: rgb(0,128,128);">Boolean</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">;<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">    }<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">}</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);">package<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,128,128);">Vehicle</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> {</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">    </span>private<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span>import<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,128,128);">MetadataDefinitions</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">    </span>part<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">vehicle</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> {</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">        </span>part<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">interior</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> {</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">            </span>part<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">alarm</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">;</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">            </span>part<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,17,136);">seatBelt</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> [</span><span style="color: rgb(9,134,88);">2</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">] {</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">                @</span><span style="color: rgb(0,128,128);">Safety</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">{</span><span style="color:
````
