<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304015534 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00533: COPY Tabular view columns

- page_id: `304015534`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304015534/COPY+Tabular+view+columns
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-05-06T16:58:41.786+02:00`
- ancestors: COPY Creating tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Tabular views contain columns used to represent specific information about the exposed elements. See the information on this page to learn about table columns before you start [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Modeling tabular view columns' space=''].

##### Columns in tabular view definitions and usages

Tabular views and their definitions can differ in the table columns they contain:

- Tabular view definitions:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''] come with predefined columns.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Custom tabular view definitions' space=''] contain only the columns you specify.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COPY Creating tabular views' space=''] inherit columns from the view definitions they are defined by. You can also specify custom columns for them.

##### Column kinds

Currently, there are 2 model-based table column kinds available for modeling. The columns you create for a tabular view usage or definition must be defined by one of these column definitions, depending on the column's purpose. The table below lists:

- Column kinds and their descriptions.
- Relevant information regarding cell type according to column kind.
  - The cell type enables correct data representation and correct cell value type (e.g., String, Boolean, etc.) suggestions upon cell editing.
- Predefined attributes applicable to each column kind.

| Column | Description | Cells | Cell attributes | Column attributes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ColumnByFeatureView | Dedicated to meta-feature, or user-defined feature, value retrieval. It requires the expression to return only one meta-feature/user-defined feature, e.g., Element::declaredName or FeatureValue::value. The expression is specified for columnFeature.>]]> | For ColumnByFeatureView, the cell type is calculated out of the meta-feature or user-defined feature and does not need to be specified explicitly. | maxDisplayedValues. It allows you to modify the default limit for displayed values in multi-valued table cells.Available values: Integer. If undefined or <=0, the attribute reverts to the default value.The default value is 10. >]]> | isMultiline. It allows you to specify whether single-valued string cells should be displayed in a multiline format.Available values: Boolean.The default value is false.pinning. It allows you to pin the column to the left or the right side of the table.Available values: left/right.By default, no column is pinned.sorting. It allows you to sort table rows in ascending or descending order according to the selected column. If the sorting is specified for multiple columns, use the sortingOrder attribute to specify the sorting order priority for the columns.Available values: asc/desc. If undefined or null, the column is not sorted.By default, the attribute value is undefined.sortingOrder. It allows you to specify the sorting order priority for the columns with the sorting attribute. The table rows are ordered for all columns based on the order specified using this attribute, e.g., a column with sortingOrder set to 0 will take the highest priority.If the sorting attribute is specified but the sortingOrder is not, sorting is applied without any order. Available values: Integer.By default, the attribute value is undefined.width. It allows you to specify the column width, where the integer value is interpreted in pixels. Available values: Integer. If undefined or null, the column width is responsive.By default, the attribute value is undefined.>]]> |
| ColumnByExpressionView | Dedicated to complex model queries. For example, when the value of the owned attribute needs to be retrieved, the feature chain expression is needed to access this information.>]]> | For ColumnByExpressionView, the cell type is specified by defining the cell rendering with the appropriate rendering definition. It works as a kind of filter for the type of wanted expression results.The default cell rendering definition is StringCellRendering.Rendering definitionsrendering def BooleanCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def ElementCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def EnumCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def IntegerCellRendering :> TableCellRendering; rendering def MetaobjectCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def RealCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def StringCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def TableCellRendering { calc getValue { in rowElement [0..1] : Metaobjects::Metaobject; } attribute isMultiValued [0..1] : ScalarValues::Boolean; } |  |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">Tabular views contain columns <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a43b3e01-79af-434e-9dda-e1791f97df98">used to represent specific information about the exposed elements</ac:inline-comment-marker>. See the information on this page to learn about table columns before you start <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[modeling them]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Columns in tabular view definitions and usages</h3><p style="text-align: left;">Tabular views and their definitions can differ in the table columns they contain:</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="3ec94e1c-e1e5-45f5-93a9-452bcb101955">Tabular view definitions:<ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="cf4fd0b4-cf7c-4e77-a82b-9ae39821bac9"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Predefined tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> come with predefined columns.</li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="a1e61253-c114-4172-9b26-27e4819b9ceb"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Custom tabular view definitions" /></ac:link> contain only the columns you specify. </li></ul></li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="e485e8a4-807d-4d11-9586-878b40e68064"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular view usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> inherit columns from the view definitions they are defined by. You can also specify custom columns for them.</li></ul><h3 style="text-align: left;">Column kinds</h3><p style="text-align: left;">Currently, there are 2 model-based table column kinds available for modeling. The columns you create for a tabular view usage or definition must be defined by one of these column definitions, depending on the column's purpose. The table below lists:</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="5df54d9b-131e-4645-abd4-b5b1dc18380e">Column kinds and their descriptions.</li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="ba5b3521-0c18-45e8-b7e4-fb4b5ca0a431">Relevant information regarding cell type according to column kind.<br /><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="5d2bc526-c693-4cd4-b59e-066d811217b8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The cell type enables correct data representation and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="00a78496-3366-4701-b4cf-6b2f70e0c1bb">correct cell value type (e.g., String, Boolean, etc.) suggestions upon cell editing.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li></ul></li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="1defea6a-2f57-4371-9820-62e256f8c265">Predefined attributes applicable to each column kind.</li></ul><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Column</th><th scope="col">Description</th><th scope="col">Cells</th><th scope="col">Cell attributes</th><th scope="col">Column attributes</th></tr><tr><td><em>ColumnByFeatureView</em></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">Dedicated to meta-feature, or user-defined feature, value retrieval. It requires the expression to return only one meta-feature/user-defined feature, e.g., </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">Element::declaredName </ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">or </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">FeatureValue::value</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">. The expression is specified for </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">columnFeature</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by feature"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See procedure >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></td><td><ul><li data-uuid="250b3a27-0a06-4f19-84f9-e753e9ee9773"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>For <em>ColumnByFeatureView</em>, the cell type is calculated out of the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="250e72b1-5f1b-468a-8555-495793ba82f6"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a0670dd4-96e3-4cbe-8b1b-30ab4b63c96b">meta-feature or user-defined feature</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker> </span>and does not need to be specified explicitly.</span></span></li></ul></td><td rowspan="2"><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><em>maxDisplayedValues</em>. It allows you to modify the default limit for displayed values in multi-valued table cells.</span></span><ul><li data-uuid="b8f1362d-9a81-4f7c-a13e-2639e770d047"><span>Available values</span>: Integer. If undefined or &lt;=0, the attribute reverts to the default value.</li><li data-uuid="1838f934-d3fa-4c24-901b-dd0a4a2fe6ee"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The default value is 10. <br /><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the element count in multi-valued cells"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See procedure >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></li></ul></li></ul><div class="content-wrapper"><span> </span></div></td><td rowspan="2"><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li><span><em>isMultiline</em>. It allows you to specify whether single-valued string cells should be displayed in a multiline format.</span><ul><li data-uuid="bc29d39f-3592-4062-911f-fccc9f984e88"><span>Available values: Boolean.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="e5a1a894-050a-4aea-9e39-030b46fca172"><span>The default value is <em>false</em>.<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="050b80f8-0957-4889-a70c-e218a53a2d56"><span><em>pinning. </em>It allows you to pin the column to the left or the right side of the table.</span><ul><li data-uuid="464e3604-b298-42ce-bae4-9a7de24e0fea"><span>Available values: <em>left/right</em>.</span></li><li data-uuid="2ec08fba-92ec-40b7-913d-ea770e13a5a3"><span>By default, no column is pinned.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="8044834c-b16f-4910-aed0-925d09c8e144"><span><em>sorting. </em>It allows you to sort table rows in ascending or descending order according to the selected column. </span><ul><li data-uuid="47ee0e81-841b-4d04-998a-92fc618f606b"><span>If the sorting is specified for multiple columns, use the <em>sortingOrder </em>attribute to specify the sorting order priority for the columns.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="4d754b88-2e74-4f38-80ff-27472041ea45"><span>Available values: <em>asc/desc</em>. </span>If undefined or <em>null</em>, the column is not sorted.</li><li data-uuid="8044834c-b16f-4910-aed0-925d09c8e144"><span>By default, the attribute value is undefined.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b142151b-568b-4e12-840a-f6cfee9f6126"><span><em>sortingOrder. </em>It allows you to specify the sorting order priority for the columns with the <em>sorting </em>attribute. The table rows are ordered for all columns based on the order specified using this attribute, e.g., a column with <em>sortingOrder </em>set to <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7ba171af-9fb8-46af-850c-44ef9e0f8a2e">0</ac:inline-comment-marker> will take the highest priority.</span><ul><li data-uuid="0b30f7da-bc77-4c06-9594-3ed99b9d2457"><span>If the <em>sorting </em>attribute is specified but the <em>sortingOrder </em>is not, <em>sorting </em>is applied without any order. </span></li><li data-uuid="fd67a89a-2785-4618-84f2-788ab3b58952"><span>Available values</span>: Integer.</li><li data-uuid="ac1efaa8-c5f9-4a64-b78c-1fca1beb6ed3"><span>By default, the attribute value is undefined.</span></li></ul></li></ul><ul><li data-uuid="b705a1a7-8a59-479e-8d58-a4b2bcd2450a"><span><em>width.</em> It allows you to specify the column width, where the integer value is interpreted in pixels. </span><ul><li data-uuid="dd35de50-396d-48f9-b703-4277309c7854"><span>Available values</span>: Integer. If undefined or <em>null</em>, the column width is responsive.</li><li data-uuid="6fc21655-e1f7-43b7-a323-d261ad48aa34"><span>By default, the attribute value is undefined.</span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying table column attributes"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See procedure >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><em>ColumnByExpressionView</em></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Dedicated to complex model queries. For example, when the value of the owned attribute needs to be retrieved, the feature chain expression is needed to access this information.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by expression"><ri:page ri:content-title="COPY Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See procedure >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li data-uuid="3dec134c-fe7c-4ffb-8ebd-e3facc5f3402"><span>For <em>ColumnByExpressionView</em>, the cell type is specified by defining the cell rendering with the appropriate rendering definition. It works as a kind of filter for the type of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2e4eec76-dcf8-425b-bad2-7d6ef344d3c5">wanted expression results</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br /></span><ul><li data-uuid="1a979d84-132b-43f5-b781-82c63a6c75ae"><span>The default cell rendering definition is <em>StringCellRendering</em>.</span></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4f5ac18-8795-4230-a5e5-cab99bbec49b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Rendering definitions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d29fa686-5aed-4181-8547-e15c52fcd897"><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">BooleanCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ElementCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">EnumCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">IntegerCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetaobjectCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">RealCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StringCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;<br /></span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TableCellRendering</span><span> </span>{<br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getValue</span><span> </span>{<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span> </span>[<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span>] : Metaobjects::Metaobject; <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125596 space=SYSML2P version=13 -->
````
## PAGE 00534: Creating a style sheet condition

- page_id: `296125596`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296125596/Creating+a+style+sheet+condition
- version_number: 13
- version_date: `2026-05-22T10:38:40.214+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Symbol styles > Custom style sheets > Creating style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify a condition that applies to the entire style sheet. All individual rules declared in the style sheet apply only to the symbols that comply with the condition specified for the entire style sheet.

- If you specify a condition for the entire style sheet, ensure the style sheet is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying style sheets' space=''] , not a single view, for the condition to take effect.
- Currently, the style sheet condition can only be created via the Textual Editor.

###### Creating a style sheet condition

To create a condition for an entire style sheet

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating style sheets' space=''] in the Textual Editor.
2. Within the body of the part definition of a style sheet, redefine the *styleSheetCondition*element inherited from the StyleSheet element and define it by the needed condition.
3. Within the body of the condition, declare a ref item , then r edefine the needed item and specify its values. The available ref items and their value types depend on the condition definition you are using. For a list of the condition definitions and their available ref items, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Style sheet styles tables' space=''].
4. You can now:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating rules for a style sheet' space=''] .
    1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] .
    2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] .
  2. Synchronize modifications and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space=''].

partdefNewStyleSheet:>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet{ 
 part:>>styleSheetCondition:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::ByDiagramTypeCondition{// style sheet condition targeting symbols by view definition 
refitem:>>diagramType=DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv.metadata;// the condition targets views defined by the *General View* view definition 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577540 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00535: Creating actions

- page_id: `249577540`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577540/Creating+actions
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-30T14:53:56.434+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Behavior > Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An action definition is a kind of occurrence definition that defines action performances. An action usage is a kind of occurrence usage that is a usage of one or more action definitions performed in a specific context. Actions coordinate the performance of other actions and generate effects on items and parts involved in the performance.

##### Creating action usage and definition elements

###### Creating action usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create action usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *action def*for an action definition.
  2. action for an action usage.
2. Specify the element name .
3. (Optional) Specify the action's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

actiondefTakePicture;// action definition

actiontakePicture:TakePicture;// action usage

###### Creating action usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create action usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element submenu, select one of the following:
  1. action def to create an action definition element.
  2. action to create an action usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the action's features.

###### Creating action usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create action usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **action** def button to create an action definition element.
  2. The **action** button to create an action usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **action**or the **action def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the action symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the action's features.

###### Creating action usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] page.

To create action usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an action symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the action element.
  - Select an action symbol and on the right side of the actions compartment, click the Create action button. The new element is created and displayed in the actions compartment.

###### Creating action usage elements via the Specification panel

To create action usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **action**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **actions**compartment section's bar, click the **Create action**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **actions** compartment.

###### Auto-creating action definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create action definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Auto-creating action usage elements via the Extract Usage commands

To auto-create action usage elements via the Extract Usage commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An action definition is a kind of occurrence definition that defines action performances. An action usage is a kind of occurrence usage that is a usage of one or more action definitions performed in a specific context. Actions coordinate the performance of other actions and generate effects on items and parts involved in the performance. </p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating action usage and definition elements</h3><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating action usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create action usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr an action definition.</p></li><li> <em>action </em>for an action usage.</li></ol></li><li><span>Specify the element name</span>.</li><li>(Optional) Specify the action's features in the element <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577736 space=SYSML2P version=11 -->
````
## PAGE 00536: Creating custom symbolic view definitions

- page_id: `249577736`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577736/Creating+custom+symbolic+view+definitions
- version_number: 11
- version_date: `2026-04-16T08:33:05.193+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Creating views > Symbolic views > Custom symbolic view definitions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the following procedures for custom symbolic view definition creation with custom and/or inherited view palettes, and predefined and/or custom templated buttons in custom and/or inherited button categories.

The procedures on this page use the example of creating custom symbolic view definitions by customizing the*DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv***standard predefined symbolic view definition. This is the recommended workflow for creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''], as the *Base Symbolic View* definition carries the predefined view palette, which contains Selection, Tools, and Common button categories, as well as the rendering feature, providing a flexible basis for creating custom symbolic view definitions.However, you can similarly customize any other symbolic view definition, whether [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbolic views' space=''] or custom.

#### NOTE: Updating custom view definition

Updating custom view definition

Reload the project for changes made to the custom symbolic view definition to take effect.

##### Creating custom symbolic view definitions

To create custom symbolic view definitions

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Textual Editor' space=''] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing and filtering elements' space=''] the *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette* package.
5. Declare a view definition, specify the name for the new symbolic view definition, subclassify the Base Symbolic View definition from the DS_Views::CoreViews package, then within the body of the view definition.
6. Click the Synchronize button. The new view definition becomes available in theonce you:[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating View Creation dialog' space=''].Add the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating View Creation dialog' space='']. This requires you to .

package'Custom symbolic view definitions' { // package for the custom symbolic view definition(s)privateimportDS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette::*; 
viewdef'Requirements View':>DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ // new custom symbolic view definition 'Requirements View' subclassifying'bsv', i.e., *'Base Symbolic View'*definition 
// specify the view palette here}}

##### Creating palettes for custom symbolic view definitions

To create palettes for custom symbolic view definitions

1. Within the body of the view definition element of the new symbolic view definition, redefine the baseViewPalette inherited from the Base Symbolic View definition.
2. Specify button categories by doing any of the following:
  1. 
  2. 
3. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ // new custom symbolic view definition 'Requirements View'part:>>baseViewPalette{ // redefines the *baseViewPalette* inherited from the *Base Symbolic View* definition. The custom palette now inherits Selection, Tools, and Common button categories// specify button categorieshere} 
}

##### Managing button categories in custom symbolic view palettes

The procedures in this subsection explain how to manage inherited and custom button categories and their attributes.

###### Removing inherited button categories

To remove inherited button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining *baseViewPalette.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name (optional), and then redefine the button category you want to remove. The button categories that are inherited from the *baseViewPalette* are *selectionCategory*, *toolsCategory*, and *commonCategory*. You can find them in the *DS_Views::ViewPalettes*package. You can also find the button categories in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''].
3. Set the redefining button category's multiplicity to zero.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{

part:>> toolsCategory[0];// unnamed part redefines *toolsCategory*inherited via *baseViewPalette,*specifying the redefining button category's multiplicity to zero to remove it} 
}

###### Adding custom button categories

To add custom button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the element redefining *baseViewPalette.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, and then subset *buttonCategories*.
3. Within the new button category's body, do any of the following:
  1. .
  2. .
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories; // new button category instance} 
}

###### Specifying attributes for custom or inherited button categories

To specify attributes for custom or inherited button categories

1. Within the body of the button category element, redefine the attributes that you want to modify with the needed values. All button categories - inherited and custom ones - inherit all the same button category attributes from *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Components::AbstractItem* and *ItemsCategory*, which are: *isCollapsed*, *hasVisibleLabels*, *label*, *description.*You can also find the attributes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''].
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ 
part:>>baseViewPalette{ 
// modifying an inherited button category's attributes: 
part:>>selectionCategory{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Selection";// modified category name 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaulttrue;// modified category default collapse state 
} 
// specifying the custom button category's attributes: 
partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories{ 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaultfalse;// newly specified *isCollapsed* attribute for the custom button category 
attribute:>>hasVisibleLabelsdefaulttrue;// newly specified *hasVisibleLabels*attribute for the custom button category 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Requirements";// newly specified *label*attribute for the custom button category 
attribute:>>descriptiondefault"Requirements";// newly specified *description*attribute for the custom button category 
}} 
}

##### Managing buttons in button categories

A button category can contain specific and/or menu buttons that contain specific buttons in its submenu. You can also specify one of the submenu buttons as the focused button that would be displayed as the default one for the menu button. Additionally, you can create templates for custom buttons, allowing you to add custom templated buttons to custom and/or inherited button categories.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the custom button category 'Requirements' that contains multiple category-specific buttons. 'requirement' (as well as 'software requirement' and 'constraint') is a menu button with submenu buttons. 
The 'requirement' button is displayed as the default one for the menu button.

###### Creating menu buttons in button categories

To create menu buttons in button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the button category element*.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, define it by *ButtonsCategory,* and then subset *abstractButtons*.
3. Add submenu buttons for the menu button:
  1. .
  2. .
4. (Optional) Specify the focused button:
  1. Redefine *focusedButton*, and specify the focused button by indicating the name of the needed button's part element.
5. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories {

partreqButtonMenu:ButtonsCategory:>abstractButtons{ // menu button instance 
partreqButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button

performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in ref= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementAction;}} 
partreqDefButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in ref= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementDefAction;} 
} 
part:>>focusedButton=reqButton; // the part *reqButton* is specified as the focused (default) button for the menu button 
}}} 
}

###### Adding predefined buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

To add predefined buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

1. Do one of the following:
  1. To create specific buttons, place the cursor within the body of the button category element.
  2. To create submenu buttons for the menu button, place the cursor within the body of the menu button element.
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, define it by *Button,* and then subset *abstractButtons*.
3. Within the body of the part, declare a perform action, define it by *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode,* and subset *operation*. In the element's body, specify the *in*direction with the code action identifier based on the type of element you want the button to create. You can find the needed code action identifier for the element kind you want the button to create in the *DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers*package. It looks like this:attributerequirementAction : ScalarValues::String="ADD_REQUIREMENT"; // the code action identifier for the requirement usage element button is *requirementAction*
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories {

partreqButtonMenu:ButtonsCategory:>abstractButtons{ // menu button instance 
partreqButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button

performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in ref= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementAction;} // specifies *requirementAction* so that this button creates the requirement usage element} 
partreqDefButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in ref= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementDefAction;} // specifies *requirementDefAction* so that this button creates the requirement definition element 
} 
part:>>focusedButton=reqButton; 
}}} 
}

###### Removing buttons from inherited button categories

To remove buttons from inherited button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the element redefining *baseViewPalette.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name (optional), and then redefine the button you want to remove. See the buttons specified for each predefined button category in the *DS_Views::ViewPalettes*package. You can also find it in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''].
3. Set the redefining button's multiplicity to zero.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{

part:>>selectionCategory{ // unnamed part redefines *selectionCategory*inherited via *baseViewPalette*

// ------ predefined button:partcompleteCoverageModeButton :DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette::Button:> abstractButtons

// ------ modified button (instead of the notation above):part:>>completeCoverageModeButton[0]; // multiplicity for the button *completeCoverageModeButton*is set to zero to remove it}} 
}

##### Managing custom templated buttons in button categories

Instead of or in addition to using predefined buttons either by adding them to your custom or inherited button categories, you can also create your own custom buttons. For that, first, you need to create a template for the custom button, and then you can add it to a specific button category of the view palette.

###### Creating templates for custom buttons

The following procedure depicts how to create an element template using metadata to extend the requirement usage element with the needed attribute, where the requirement usage element is named *Software*. Then the template can be passed into the custom button specification using *metadataAccessExpression*.

To create templates for custom buttons

1. Within the package containing the new symbolic view definition, d eclare:
  1. A metadata definition element with the needed name and attributes.
  2. A package element for the template.
    1. Within the template element, apply metadata to the element kind you want to extend and specify the element name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

package'Custom symbolic view definitions'{

// template for a custom button *softwareRequirement*:

metadatadefSoftware{// declared metadata definition with the needed attribute 
attributeverificationMethod[0..*] :VerificationMethod; 
} 
packagesoftwareReqTemplate{// custom button template 
#SoftwarerequirementsoftwareRequirement;// the metadata Software is applied to the requirement usage element extending it with a new name Software and a metadata attribute. The name softwareRequirement will be used for the perform action's element specification to pass the template to a button in a button category 
}

// template for a templated button *satisfy*:

packageSatisfyReqTemplate{ 
usecaseuseCase1{ 
satisfyrequirement1; 
} 
requirementrequirement1; 
} 
}

###### Adding custom templated buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

To add custom templated buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

1. Do one of the following:
  1. To create specific buttons, place the cursor within the body of the button category element.
  2. To create submenu buttons for the menu button, place the cursor within the body of the menu button element.
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, define it by *Button,* and then subset *abstractButtons*.
3. Within the body of the part, specify attributes for the custom templated button:
  1. Redefine the label attribute and specify the button name.
  2. Redefine the description attribute and specify the button's tooltip.
  3. Redefine the representation attribute and specify how the element should be represented in a view. Available *representation* attribute value options:*shape*- regular element symbol as a shape.*shapeOnBorder*- port or other directional feature representation.*path*- relationship element representation.*naryShape*- n-relationship element representation.Note:Relationships can be displayed as paths or n-ary shapes (e.g., [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='N-ary connection' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='N-ary interface' space='']).Ports or other directional features can be displayed as shapes or shapes on a shape's border.Referential elements can be displayed as shapes or paths (e.g., [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Perform action' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibit state' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Include use case' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Satisfy requirement' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assume constraint' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Require constraint' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assert constraint' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Frame concern' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Occurrences' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rendering views basics' space='']).The attribute can only be applied to *OperationFromTemplate* buttons. The specified representation value is ignored if it is unapplicable to the templated element.
4. Declare a perform action, define it by *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate,* and subset *operation*. In the element's body, specify the *in* direction with the custom button's template path using *metadataAccessExpression* . A *metadataAccessExpression* must be used for the *in*element. Its result must be exactly one templated element that you want to copy for the button.Alternatively, you can also use metaclassification expressions, e.g., when the templated element is unnamed. The expression would be: *(**softwareReqTemplate meta KerML::Element).ownedElement*
5. Click the Synchronize button.

package'Custom symbolic view definitions'{ 
privateimportDS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette::*; 
viewdef'Requirements View':>DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ 
part:>>baseViewPalette{ 
partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories{

partsoftwareButton:Button:>abstractButtons{// menu button instance 
attribute:>>label="software requirement";// specify the button name 
attribute:>>description="software requirement";// specify the button tooltip 
// the *representation* attribute not specified because the requirement element can only be represented as a shape 
// *OperationFromTemplate* specifies that the indicated button is templated; in specifies the created element, which is *softwareRequirement* (which also contains metadata) from the template *softwareReqTemplate*: 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>operation{ 
inref=softwareReqTemplate::softwareRequirement.metadata; 
} 
} 
partsatisfyPath:Button:>abstractButtons{ 
attribute:>>label="satisfy path"; 
attribute:>>representation=representation::path;// representation specified as 'path' (*satisfy*can be path or shape) 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{ 
inref= (SatisfyReqTemplate::useCase1metaKerML::Root::Element).ownedElementasSysML::Systems::SatisfyRequirementUsage; 
} 
} 
} 
} 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>See the following procedures for custom symbolic view definition creation with custom and/or inherited view palettes, and predefined and/or custom templated buttons in custom and/or inherited button categories.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f00e7e31-7544-4226-924b-0c0f8dbb802c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The procedures on this page use the example of creating custom symbolic view definitions by customizing the<span> <em>DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv</em></span><em><span> </span></em>standard predefined symbolic view definition. This is the recommended workflow for <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>creating <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom symbolic view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom symbolic view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, as the <em>Base Symbolic View</em> definition carries the predefined view palette, which contains</span> Selection, Tools, and Common button categories, as well as the rendering feature, providing a flexible basis for creating custom symbolic view definitions. </span>However, you can similarly customize any other symbolic view definition, whether <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[predefined]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or custom.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d9582977-705b-4e53-a466-e41c137ce130"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating custom view definition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Reload the project for changes made to the custom symbolic view definition to take effect.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Creating custom symbolic view definitions</span></h3><p>To c<span>reate custom symbolic view definitions</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="591e0035-5a7d-4503-9683-47dae37c3a23">In the Containment tree,<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Declaring a root namespace"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a new root namespace]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="2842cfaa-a826-4f1a-acc2-597df20985c6"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>for the new namespace.</li><li data-uuid="06cc0318-df7b-4184-bf0a-f3336ab850b2">In the Textual Editor,<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a package element via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>and name it.</li><li data-uuid="c542e40a-270a-4f17-aab7-b42641ea7657">Within the body of the package,<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing and filtering elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>the<span> <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette</em></span> package.</li><li data-uuid="7b2f0f07-e130-4649-a857-19e12be3dc92">Declare a view definition, specify the name for the new symbolic view definition, subclassify the <em>Base Symbolic View</em> definition from the <em>DS_Views::CoreViews </em>package, then <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating palettes for custom symbolic view definitions"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a custom view palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> within the body of the view definition.</li><li data-uuid="f2707b21-288a-4fb0-b799-44dc703e5a4d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="954b0db7-4aeb-4978-8a86-cfa4c049770e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The new view definition becomes available <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">in the<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[View Creation dialog/submenu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> once you:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating view templates for custom templated commands"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a view template for a custom templated command]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Add the </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom templated command to the View Creation dialog/submenu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This requires you to <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customize or create a View Creation dialog customization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac52faf4-2a1b-4b92-ac5e-65a9c42e9b9a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Custom symbolic view definitions' {   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// package for the custom symbolic view definition(s)</span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   <u>view</u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>'Requirements View'<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span></u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> // <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">new custom symbolic view definition 'Requirements View' subclassifying </span>'bsv', i.e., <em>'Base Symbolic View' </em>definition</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">       // specify the view palette here</span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span>}<br /></span>}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Creating palettes for custom symbolic view definitions</span></h3><p>To create palettes for custom symbolic view definitions</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5bc73da9-4829-4341-b014-e81645757d3e">Within the body of the view definition element of the new symbolic view definition, redefine the <em>baseViewPalette </em>inherited from the <em>Base Symbolic View</em> definition.</li><li data-uuid="412ef32a-2f3d-4063-9dc8-2529a8d30b9f">Specify button categories by doing any of the following:<ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Removing inherited button categories"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remove inherited button categories]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding custom button categories"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add custom button categories]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="ec6f71ba-1335-4cf9-9098-64f153a23236"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9223faa-0c80-44cf-af4a-6cdb8d5ce433"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Requirements View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new custom symbolic view definition 'Requirements View'<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    <u>part</u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">baseViewPalette</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {     <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// redefines the <em>baseViewPalette</em> inherited from the <em>Base Symbolic View</em> definition. The custom palette now inherits Selection, Tools, and Common button categories</span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        // specify button categories<span> here</span></span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }<br />}<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing button categories in custom symbolic view palettes</h3><p>The procedures in this subsection explain how to manage inherited and custom button categories and their attributes.</p><h4>Removing inherited button categories</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To remove inherited button categories</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="520c97cc-5516-4917-99fd-f8cb8fd51722"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining <em>baseViewPalette.</em></span></li><li data-uuid="a6db4cdb-169a-4c1d-bddd-5c5d380c8dff"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a part usage, specify the element name (optional), and then redefine the button category you want to remove.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69a869db-c56f-47bc-9fb5-dd9e448a09bf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The button categories that are inherited from the <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">baseViewPalette</span></em> are <em>selectionCategory</em>, <em>toolsCategory</em>, and <em>commonCategory</em>. You can find them in the <em>DS_Views::ViewPalettes </em>package. You can also find the button categories in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom symbolic view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[General button categories table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="41122608-8328-42cb-b9b6-a806d5aa075a">Set the redefining button category's multiplicity to zero.</li><li data-uuid="3c66e14e-5aa1-4660-a482-26bb6bca5e41"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="931651bd-cb55-4752-98f0-162aa5b32ecb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Requirements View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">baseViewPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {     <br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">        <u>part</u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; toolsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> [0];</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed part redefines <em>toolsCategory </em>inherited via <em>baseViewPalette, </em>specifying the redefining button category's multiplicity to zero to remove it</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }<br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Adding custom button categories</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To add custom button categories</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="4aa7eeca-f25c-4e9e-bc8d-24da724e0099"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Place the cursor within the body of the element redefining <em>baseViewPalette.</em></span></li><li data-uuid="bfe6f984-9ca9-4c94-be8f-eae1bdd37655"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a part usage, specify the element name, and then subset <em>buttonCategories</em>.</span></li><li data-uuid="d7b0e2c8-2ea5-4de6-b41e-98f680ac80e5">Within the new button category's body, do any of the following:<ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying attributes for custom or inherited button categories"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the button category attributes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Managing buttons in button categories"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the button category buttons]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="5bfacd96-f523-4cbb-a993-d0818e4f63a8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8f6bcc3-35bd-4427-934c-ead5736f2ad9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Requirements View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">baseViewPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {    <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">        <u>part</u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">requirementsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><u>buttonCategories</u>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new button category instance</span></span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }<br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying attributes for custom or inherited button categories</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To specify attributes for custom or inherited button categories</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1d067d54-5769-4531-9ae9-86b3955a1b97"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the button category element, redefine the </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9b8f189-771e-499e-9df4-66ca2910862e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All button categories - inherited and custom ones - inherit all the same button category attributes from <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Components::AbstractItem</em> and <em>ItemsCategory</em>, which are: <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>isCollapsed</em>, <em>hasVisibleLabels</em>, <em>label</em>, <em>description. </em></span>You can also find the attributes in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom symbolic view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[General button categories table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="6936a4fa-8aa7-4e8e-b2f5-8981a817faed"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e7b34c2-115e-43b3-8421-48a3c792679a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Requirements View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">baseViewPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// modifying an inherited button category's attributes:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">selectionCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Selection&quot;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// modified category name</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isCollapsed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// modified category default collapse state</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">       }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the custom button category's attributes:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">requirementsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">buttonCategories</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isCollapsed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// newly specified <em>isCollapsed</em> attribute <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="42921599-9fc4-4ba2-9683-4573a31932d7">for the custom button category</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         <u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span></u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hasVisibleLabels</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// newly specified <em>hasVisibleLabels </em>attribute for the custom button category</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Requirements&quot;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// newly specified <em>label </em>attribute for the custom button category</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">description</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Requirements&quot;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// newly specified <em>description </em>attribute for the custom button category</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }  <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing buttons in button categories</h3><p>A button category can contain specific and/or menu buttons that contain specific buttons in its submenu. You can also specify one of the submenu buttons as the focused button that would be displayed as the default one for the menu button. Additionally, you can create templates for custom buttons, allowing you to add custom templated buttons to custom and/or inherited button categories.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_palette_composition.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays the custom button category 'Requirements' that contains multiple category-specific buttons. 'requirement' (as well as 'software requirement' and 'constraint') is a menu button with submenu buttons. <br />The 'requirement' button is displayed as the default one for the menu button.   </h6><h4>Creating menu buttons in button categories</h4><p>To create menu buttons in button categories</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6f33e257-f9f8-46c6-966a-1c1d4922e8ec"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Place the cursor within the body of the button category element<em>.</em></span></li><li data-uuid="2344ba37-21a9-48b5-9ffc-40ba6f212bda"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a part usage, specify the element name, define it by <em>ButtonsCategory,</em> and then subset <em>abstractButtons</em>.</span></li><li data-uuid="1396b09d-d41b-488b-9044-9ceb9eccd62a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Add submenu buttons for the menu button:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding predefined buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add predefined buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding custom templated buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add custom templated buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="8655d7e0-07ed-4bbb-8a28-4fe20779279b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(Optional) Specify the focused button:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Redefine <em>focusedButton</em>, and specify the focused button by indicating the name of the needed button's part element.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="f839d494-71ce-4664-9010-27a6f4cf169b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1537f3f-a98a-46e1-a374-d9ff0430a2c4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7244ea5a-04f7-41a9-a5ff-849321f916b3">view</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7244ea5a-04f7-41a9-a5ff-849321f916b3"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7244ea5a-04f7-41a9-a5ff-849321f916b3">def</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7244ea5a-04f7-41a9-a5ff-849321f916b3"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7244ea5a-04f7-41a9-a5ff-849321f916b3">'Requirements View'</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7244ea5a-04f7-41a9-a5ff-849321f916b3"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7244ea5a-04f7-41a9-a5ff-849321f916b3"> {</ac:inline-comment-marker>  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">baseViewPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {    <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">        part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">requirementsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">buttonCategories {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">            <u>part</u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">reqButtonMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ButtonsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">abstractButtons</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// menu button instance</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">reqButton</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Button</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">abstractButtons</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// submenu button</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">operation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b61030fa-8ee9-4585-a05f-1d2555a1cbb4">in ref</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">requirementAction</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">reqDefButton</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Button</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">abstractButtons</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// submenu button</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode</span><span
````
